title: Principles relating to processing of personal data
summary: States the GDPR's core processing principles, expressly including lawfulness, fairness, purpose limitation, data minimisation, storage limitation, security, and accountability.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in gdpr-articles.json.

source.label: EUR-Lex

editorial.note: Concept tags identify express principles in the Article; they do not establish equivalence with another instrument.

title: Lawfulness of processing
summary: Identifies the conditions under which processing is lawful, including consent and five other legal bases, and sets a compatibility analysis for certain further processing.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in gdpr-articles.json.

source.label: EUR-Lex

editorial.note: Consent is one legal basis among several; this concept tag does not present it as the default basis.

title: Processing of special categories of personal data
summary: Prohibits processing specified special categories of personal data unless one of the Article's enumerated conditions and safeguards applies.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in gdpr-articles.json.

source.label: EUR-Lex

editorial.note: The GDPR's enumerated special categories should not be treated as a universal definition of sensitive data.

title: Transparent information, communication and modalities for the exercise of the rights of the data subject
summary: Sets transparency, communication, timing, identity-verification, and procedural requirements for notices and the exercise of GDPR data-subject rights.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in gdpr-articles.json.

source.label: EUR-Lex

editorial.note: The Article supplies modalities for specified GDPR rights rather than a jurisdiction-neutral request procedure.

title: Right of access by the data subject
summary: Gives data subjects a qualified right to confirmation, access, specified processing information, and a copy of personal data undergoing processing.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in gdpr-articles.json.

source.label: EUR-Lex

editorial.note: Scope, verification, copies, third-party rights, and applicable restrictions require provision-specific analysis.

title: Right to erasure (‘right to be forgotten’)
summary: Creates a qualified right and corresponding controller duty to erase personal data when stated grounds apply, subject to enumerated exceptions.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in gdpr-articles.json.

source.label: EUR-Lex

editorial.note: The right is conditional and subject to exceptions; the tag does not imply an absolute deletion requirement.

title: Data protection by design and by default
summary: Requires risk-sensitive technical and organisational measures that implement data-protection principles in system design and ensure necessary-only processing by default.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in gdpr-articles.json.

source.label: EUR-Lex

editorial.note: Pseudonymisation is an example measure in the Article, not a universal safe harbor or complete compliance solution.

title: Processor
summary: Governs controller selection and oversight of processors, processor contracts, subprocessor authorization, assistance, return or deletion, and audit support.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in gdpr-articles.json.

source.label: EUR-Lex

editorial.note: The supply-chain tag is thematic; controller-processor roles and duties must be established under the GDPR itself.

title: Automated individual decision-making, including profiling
summary: Provides protections concerning certain decisions based solely on automated processing that produce legal or similarly significant effects, subject to stated exceptions and safeguards.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in gdpr-articles.json.

source.label: EUR-Lex

editorial.note: Summary does not reproduce statutory text or resolve CJEU interpretation.

title: Security of processing
summary: Requires controllers and processors to implement security measures appropriate to risk, informed by context, cost, and the likelihood and severity of impacts.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in gdpr-articles.json.

source.label: EUR-Lex

editorial.note: The listed measures are contextual rather than a universal control checklist.

title: Notification of a personal data breach to the supervisory authority
summary: Requires controllers to notify the competent supervisory authority of qualifying personal-data breaches, generally within 72 hours, and creates related processor and documentation duties.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in gdpr-articles.json.

source.label: EUR-Lex

editorial.note: Risk exceptions and national supervisory practice require context.

title: Data protection impact assessment
summary: Requires an impact assessment before processing that is likely to result in a high risk to individuals' rights and freedoms, including listed categories of processing.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in gdpr-articles.json.

source.label: EUR-Lex

editorial.note: Supervisory-authority lists and guidance can change the practical trigger analysis.

title: General principle for transfers
summary: Conditions transfers of personal data to third countries or international organizations on compliance with GDPR Chapter V while preserving the Regulation's level of protection.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in gdpr-articles.json.

source.label: EUR-Lex

editorial.note: A complete transfer analysis also requires the other provisions of Chapter V and current case law.

title: Risk management system
summary: Requires an iterative lifecycle risk-management system for high-risk AI systems within the Article's scope.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in eu-ai-act-articles.json.

source.label: EUR-Lex

editorial.note: Status is recorded two weeks before the general application date; Article 113 exceptions must be checked.

title: Data and data governance
summary: Sets quality and governance requirements for training, validation, and testing data sets used by high-risk AI systems, subject to its scope and exceptions.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in eu-ai-act-articles.json.

source.label: EUR-Lex

editorial.note: Requirements are specific to scoped high-risk systems and are not a general data-protection rule.

title: Transparency and provision of information to deployers
summary: Requires high-risk AI systems to be sufficiently transparent for deployers to interpret outputs and use the system appropriately, with instructions containing specified information.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in eu-ai-act-articles.json.

source.label: EUR-Lex

editorial.note: Do not conflate provider-to-deployer transparency with an individual right to explanation.

title: Accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity
summary: Requires high-risk AI systems to achieve appropriate levels of accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity and to perform consistently throughout their lifecycle.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in eu-ai-act-articles.json.

source.label: EUR-Lex

editorial.note: The applicable state of the art and system context remain material.

title: Responsibilities along the AI value chain
summary: Allocates provider responsibilities when specified parties modify or rebrand high-risk AI systems and requires cooperation and written arrangements with certain third-party suppliers.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in eu-ai-act-articles.json.

source.label: EUR-Lex

editorial.note: The thematic supply-chain tag does not erase the Article's actor-specific conditions, exceptions, or allocation rules.

title: Transparency obligations for providers and deployers of certain AI systems
summary: Creates scenario-specific duties to inform people when they interact with AI and to mark or disclose specified synthetic or manipulated content, subject to the Article's exceptions and implementation rules.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in eu-ai-act-articles.json.

source.label: EUR-Lex

editorial.note: The Article contains actor-, system-, content-, and context-specific exceptions; the concept tag is not a universal disclosure rule.

title: Post-market monitoring by providers and post-market monitoring plan for high-risk AI systems
summary: Requires proportionate, documented post-market monitoring that systematically gathers and analyses performance data throughout a high-risk AI system's lifetime to evaluate continuing compliance.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in eu-ai-act-articles.json.

source.label: EUR-Lex

editorial.note: Monitoring duties are scoped to covered systems and must be read with the Act's implementation and transition rules.

title: Entry into force and application
summary: Establishes the AI Act's entry into force, general application date, and the principal earlier and later application exceptions.

textAvailability.note: A complete official Article record is generated in eu-ai-act-articles.json.

source.label: EUR-Lex

editorial.note: The visualization should derive provision status from this phased schedule rather than a single instrument-wide boolean.

title: Reporting for dual-use foundation models and computing clusters
summary: Directed use of Defense Production Act authorities to require specified reports and information concerning certain dual-use foundation models, large computing clusters, and associated testing.

textAvailability.note: Historical text remains available from the Federal Register.

source.label: Federal Register

editorial.note: No current obligation should be inferred after revocation of the parent order.

title: Developing an Artificial Intelligence Action Plan
summary: Directed designated White House officials, in coordination with relevant agencies, to develop an action plan implementing the order's AI leadership policy.

textAvailability.note: Official text is linked from the Federal Register.

source.label: Federal Register

editorial.note: The resulting Action Plan and later executive orders are distinct instruments.

title: Automated decision-making safeguards
summary: Requires transparency, fairness, and impartiality in automated decision-making and provides specific protections concerning marketing and decisions with a major impact on personal rights and interests.
paragraphs: 个人信息处理者利用个人信息进行自动化决策，应当保证决策的透明度和结果公平、公正，不得对个人在交易价格等交易条件上实行不合理的差别待遇。
通过自动化决策方式向个人进行信息推送、商业营销，应当同时提供不针对其个人特征的选项，或者向个人提供便捷的拒绝方式。
通过自动化决策方式作出对个人权益有重大影响的决定，个人有权要求个人信息处理者予以说明，并有权拒绝个人信息处理者仅通过自动化决策的方式作出决定。
fullText:
个人信息处理者利用个人信息进行自动化决策，应当保证决策的透明度和结果公平、公正，不得对个人在交易价格等交易条件上实行不合理的差别待遇。

通过自动化决策方式向个人进行信息推送、商业营销，应当同时提供不针对其个人特征的选项，或者向个人提供便捷的拒绝方式。

通过自动化决策方式作出对个人权益有重大影响的决定，个人有权要求个人信息处理者予以说明，并有权拒绝个人信息处理者仅通过自动化决策的方式作出决定。

en.title: Automated decision-making safeguards
paragraphs: When personal information handlers use personal information to make automated decisions, they shall ensure the transparency of the decision-making and the fairness and impartiality of the results and shall not impose unreasonable differential treatment on individuals in transaction terms such as transaction prices.

When information is pushed or commercial marketing is conducted through automated decision-making, individuals shall also be given an option that is not targeted to their personal characteristics or a convenient means to refuse.

When a decision that has a significant impact on an individual's rights and interests is made through automated decision-making, the individual has the right to require the personal information handler to provide an explanation and the right to refuse a decision made by the handler solely through automated decision-making.
en.fullText:
When personal information handlers use personal information to make automated decisions, they shall ensure the transparency of the decision-making and the fairness and impartiality of the results and shall not impose unreasonable differential treatment on individuals in transaction terms such as transaction prices.

When information is pushed or commercial marketing is conducted through automated decision-making, individuals shall also be given an option that is not targeted to their personal characteristics or a convenient means to refuse.

When a decision that has a significant impact on an individual's rights and interests is made through automated decision-making, the individual has the right to require the personal information handler to provide an explanation and the right to refuse a decision made by the handler solely through automated decision-making.
en.note: Original editorial reference translation; not an official translation. The official Chinese text controls.

textAvailability.note: Official Chinese Article text is stored verbatim; the English title and summary remain editorial.

source.label: Cyberspace Administration of China

editorial.note: Terms and enforcement practice should be checked in Chinese-language authorities.

title: Mechanisms for providing personal information outside China
summary: Lists legal mechanisms for overseas provision of personal information, including security assessment, certification, standard-contract, and other authorized routes.
paragraphs: 个人信息处理者因业务等需要，确需向中华人民共和国境外提供个人信息的，应当具备下列条件之一：
（一）依照本法第四十条的规定通过国家网信部门组织的安全评估；
（二）按照国家网信部门的规定经专业机构进行个人信息保护认证；
（三）按照国家网信部门制定的标准合同与境外接收方订立合同，约定双方的权利和义务；
（四）法律、行政法规或者国家网信部门规定的其他条件。
中华人民共和国缔结或者参加的国际条约、协定对向中华人民共和国境外提供个人信息的条件等有规定的，可以按照其规定执行。
个人信息处理者应当采取必要措施，保障境外接收方处理个人信息的活动达到本法规定的个人信息保护标准。
fullText:
个人信息处理者因业务等需要，确需向中华人民共和国境外提供个人信息的，应当具备下列条件之一：

（一）依照本法第四十条的规定通过国家网信部门组织的安全评估；

（二）按照国家网信部门的规定经专业机构进行个人信息保护认证；

（三）按照国家网信部门制定的标准合同与境外接收方订立合同，约定双方的权利和义务；

（四）法律、行政法规或者国家网信部门规定的其他条件。

中华人民共和国缔结或者参加的国际条约、协定对向中华人民共和国境外提供个人信息的条件等有规定的，可以按照其规定执行。

个人信息处理者应当采取必要措施，保障境外接收方处理个人信息的活动达到本法规定的个人信息保护标准。

en.title: Mechanisms for providing personal information outside China
paragraphs: If, for business or other needs, a personal information handler genuinely needs to provide personal information outside the territory of the People's Republic of China, it shall satisfy one of the following conditions:
(1) pass a security assessment organized by the national cyberspace administration authority in accordance with Article 40 of this Law;
(2) obtain personal information protection certification from a specialized institution in accordance with the provisions of the national cyberspace administration authority;

(3) enter into a contract with the overseas recipient, using the standard contract formulated by the national cyberspace administration authority, that specifies the rights and obligations of both parties; or
(4) satisfy other conditions prescribed by laws, administrative regulations, or the national cyberspace administration authority.

Where an international treaty or agreement concluded or acceded to by the People's Republic of China contains provisions on conditions for providing personal information outside the territory, those provisions may be followed.

The personal information handler shall take necessary measures to ensure that the overseas recipient's personal information processing meets the personal information protection standards prescribed by this Law.
en.fullText:
If, for business or other needs, a personal information handler genuinely needs to provide personal information outside the territory of the People's Republic of China, it shall satisfy one of the following conditions:

(1) pass a security assessment organized by the national cyberspace administration authority in accordance with Article 40 of this Law;

(2) obtain personal information protection certification from a specialized institution in accordance with the provisions of the national cyberspace administration authority;

(3) enter into a contract with the overseas recipient, using the standard contract formulated by the national cyberspace administration authority, that specifies the rights and obligations of both parties; or

(4) satisfy other conditions prescribed by laws, administrative regulations, or the national cyberspace administration authority.

Where an international treaty or agreement concluded or acceded to by the People's Republic of China contains provisions on conditions for providing personal information outside the territory, those provisions may be followed.

The personal information handler shall take necessary measures to ensure that the overseas recipient's personal information processing meets the personal information protection standards prescribed by this Law.
en.note: Original editorial reference translation; not an official translation. The official Chinese text controls.

textAvailability.note: Official Chinese Article text is stored verbatim; subordinate transfer rules and the editorial English summary remain separate.

source.label: Cyberspace Administration of China

editorial.note: Do not infer that listed routes operate identically to GDPR Chapter V mechanisms.

title: Personal-information security measures
summary: Requires personal-information handlers to adopt measures suited to purpose, method, category, impact, and security risk, including governance and technical controls.
paragraphs: 个人信息处理者应当根据个人信息的处理目的、处理方式、个人信息的种类以及对个人权益的影响、可能存在的安全风险等，采取下列措施确保个人信息处理活动符合法律、行政法规的规定，并防止未经授权的访问以及个人信息泄露、篡改、丢失：
（一）制定内部管理制度和操作规程；
（二）对个人信息实行分类管理；
（三）采取相应的加密、去标识化等安全技术措施；
（四）合理确定个人信息处理的操作权限，并定期对从业人员进行安全教育和培训；
（五）制定并组织实施个人信息安全事件应急预案；
（六）法律、行政法规规定的其他措施。
fullText:
个人信息处理者应当根据个人信息的处理目的、处理方式、个人信息的种类以及对个人权益的影响、可能存在的安全风险等，采取下列措施确保个人信息处理活动符合法律、行政法规的规定，并防止未经授权的访问以及个人信息泄露、篡改、丢失：

（一）制定内部管理制度和操作规程；

（二）对个人信息实行分类管理；

（三）采取相应的加密、去标识化等安全技术措施；

（四）合理确定个人信息处理的操作权限，并定期对从业人员进行安全教育和培训；

（五）制定并组织实施个人信息安全事件应急预案；

（六）法律、行政法规规定的其他措施。

en.title: Personal-information security measures
paragraphs: Taking into account the purposes and methods of personal information processing, the categories of personal information, the impact on individuals' rights and interests, potential security risks, and other factors, personal information handlers shall adopt the following measures to ensure that personal information processing complies with laws and administrative regulations and to prevent unauthorized access and the leakage, alteration, or loss of personal information:
(1) formulate internal management rules and operating procedures;
(2) implement classified management of personal information;
(3) adopt corresponding security technical measures such as encryption and de-identification;
(4) reasonably determine operational permissions for personal information processing and regularly provide personnel with security education and training;
(5) formulate and organize implementation of personal information security incident-response plans; and
(6) adopt other measures prescribed by laws and administrative regulations.
en.note: Original editorial reference translation; not an official translation. The official Chinese text controls.

textAvailability.note: Official Chinese Article text is stored verbatim; the English title and summary remain editorial.

source.label: Cyberspace Administration of China

editorial.note: Control lists and risk standards may also be specified elsewhere in Chinese law and standards.

title: Personal-information protection impact assessment
summary: Requires prior personal-information protection impact assessments for listed higher-impact activities, including sensitive information, automated decisions, entrusted processing, and overseas provision.
paragraphs: 有下列情形之一的，个人信息处理者应当事前进行个人信息保护影响评估，并对处理情况进行记录：
（一）处理敏感个人信息；
（二）利用个人信息进行自动化决策；
（三）委托处理个人信息、向其他个人信息处理者提供个人信息、公开个人信息；
（四）向境外提供个人信息；
（五）其他对个人权益有重大影响的个人信息处理活动。
fullText:
有下列情形之一的，个人信息处理者应当事前进行个人信息保护影响评估，并对处理情况进行记录：

（一）处理敏感个人信息；

（二）利用个人信息进行自动化决策；

（三）委托处理个人信息、向其他个人信息处理者提供个人信息、公开个人信息；

（四）向境外提供个人信息；

（五）其他对个人权益有重大影响的个人信息处理活动。

en.title: Personal-information protection impact assessment
paragraphs: In any of the following circumstances, a personal information handler shall conduct a personal information protection impact assessment in advance and record the processing:
(1) processing sensitive personal information;
(2) using personal information for automated decision-making;
(3) entrusting personal information processing, providing personal information to another personal information handler, or making personal information public;
(4) providing personal information outside the territory; or
(5) conducting other personal information processing activities that have a significant impact on individuals' rights and interests.
en.note: Original editorial reference translation; not an official translation. The official Chinese text controls.

textAvailability.note: Official Chinese Article text is stored verbatim; Article 56 and the editorial English summary remain separate.

source.label: Cyberspace Administration of China

editorial.note: Assessment content and retention are further addressed in Article 56.

title: Personal-information incident response
summary: Requires immediate remedial measures and notice to the responsible authority and affected individuals when personal information is or may be leaked, altered, or lost, subject to stated conditions.
paragraphs: 发生或者可能发生个人信息泄露、篡改、丢失的，个人信息处理者应当立即采取补救措施，并通知履行个人信息保护职责的部门和个人。通知应当包括下列事项：
（一）发生或者可能发生个人信息泄露、篡改、丢失的信息种类、原因和可能造成的危害；
（二）个人信息处理者采取的补救措施和个人可以采取的减轻危害的措施；
（三）个人信息处理者的联系方式。
个人信息处理者采取措施能够有效避免信息泄露、篡改、丢失造成危害的，个人信息处理者可以不通知个人；履行个人信息保护职责的部门认为可能造成危害的，有权要求个人信息处理者通知个人。
fullText:
发生或者可能发生个人信息泄露、篡改、丢失的，个人信息处理者应当立即采取补救措施，并通知履行个人信息保护职责的部门和个人。通知应当包括下列事项：

（一）发生或者可能发生个人信息泄露、篡改、丢失的信息种类、原因和可能造成的危害；

（二）个人信息处理者采取的补救措施和个人可以采取的减轻危害的措施；

（三）个人信息处理者的联系方式。

个人信息处理者采取措施能够有效避免信息泄露、篡改、丢失造成危害的，个人信息处理者可以不通知个人；履行个人信息保护职责的部门认为可能造成危害的，有权要求个人信息处理者通知个人。

en.title: Personal-information incident response
paragraphs: If personal information has been or may be leaked, altered, or lost, the personal information handler shall immediately take remedial measures and notify the department performing personal information protection duties and the affected individuals. The notice shall include the following matters:
(1) the categories of information involved in the actual or potential leak, alteration, or loss, the cause, and the harm that may result;
(2) remedial measures taken by the personal information handler and measures individuals may take to mitigate harm;
(3) the personal information handler's contact information.

If measures taken by the personal information handler can effectively prevent harm from a leak, alteration, or loss, the handler may refrain from notifying individuals; if the department performing personal information protection duties considers that harm may result, it has the right to require the handler to notify individuals.
en.note: Original editorial reference translation; not an official translation. The official Chinese text controls.

textAvailability.note: Official Chinese Article text is stored verbatim; the English title and summary remain editorial.

source.label: Cyberspace Administration of China

editorial.note: No fixed GDPR-style 72-hour deadline should be inferred from this summary.

title: Legislative purpose
summary: Editorial overview of Article 1: legislative purpose. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 为了保障网络安全，维护网络空间主权和国家安全、社会公共利益，保护公民、法人和其他组织的合法权益，促进经济社会信息化健康发展，制定本法。
fullText: 为了保障网络安全，维护网络空间主权和国家安全、社会公共利益，保护公民、法人和其他组织的合法权益，促进经济社会信息化健康发展，制定本法。

en.title: Legislative purpose
paragraphs: This Law is enacted to safeguard cybersecurity; preserve cyberspace sovereignty, national security, and the public interest; protect the lawful rights and interests of citizens, legal persons, and other organizations; and promote the sound development of economic and social informatization.
en.fullText: This Law is enacted to safeguard cybersecurity; preserve cyberspace sovereignty, national security, and the public interest; protect the lawful rights and interests of citizens, legal persons, and other organizations; and promote the sound development of economic and social informatization.
en.note: Complete project-authored English reference translation aligned Article-by-Article to the current 81-Article Chinese consolidation effective 1 January 2026. It is an editorial navigation and study aid, not an official translation; the official Chinese text controls.

title: Territorial scope
summary: Editorial overview of Article 2: territorial scope. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 在中华人民共和国境内建设、运营、维护和使用网络，以及网络安全的监督管理，适用本法。
fullText: 在中华人民共和国境内建设、运营、维护和使用网络，以及网络安全的监督管理，适用本法。

en.title: Territorial scope
paragraphs: This Law applies to the construction, operation, maintenance, and use of networks within the territory of the People's Republic of China, as well as to the supervision and administration of cybersecurity.
en.fullText: This Law applies to the construction, operation, maintenance, and use of networks within the territory of the People's Republic of China, as well as to the supervision and administration of cybersecurity.

title: Leadership and overall security approach
summary: Editorial overview of Article 3: leadership and overall security approach. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 网络安全工作坚持中国共产党的领导，贯彻总体国家安全观，统筹发展和安全，推进网络强国建设。
fullText: 网络安全工作坚持中国共产党的领导，贯彻总体国家安全观，统筹发展和安全，推进网络强国建设。

en.title: Leadership and overall security approach
paragraphs: Cybersecurity work shall uphold the leadership of the Communist Party of China, implement the holistic approach to national security, coordinate development and security, and advance the building of China into a strong cyber power.
en.fullText: Cybersecurity work shall uphold the leadership of the Communist Party of China, implement the holistic approach to national security, coordinate development and security, and advance the building of China into a strong cyber power.

title: Coordinated cybersecurity and informatization development
summary: Editorial overview of Article 4: coordinated cybersecurity and informatization development. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 国家坚持网络安全与信息化发展并重，遵循积极利用、科学发展、依法管理、确保安全的方针，推进网络基础设施建设和互联互通，鼓励网络技术创新和应用，支持培养网络安全人才，建立健全网络安全保障体系，提高网络安全保护能力。
fullText: 国家坚持网络安全与信息化发展并重，遵循积极利用、科学发展、依法管理、确保安全的方针，推进网络基础设施建设和互联互通，鼓励网络技术创新和应用，支持培养网络安全人才，建立健全网络安全保障体系，提高网络安全保护能力。

en.title: Coordinated cybersecurity and informatization development
paragraphs: The State shall attach equal importance to cybersecurity and informatization development, follow the policy of active utilization, scientific development, law-based administration, and security assurance, advance the construction and interconnection of network infrastructure, encourage innovation in and application of network technologies, support the cultivation of cybersecurity professionals, establish and improve the cybersecurity assurance system, and strengthen cybersecurity protection capabilities.
en.fullText: The State shall attach equal importance to cybersecurity and informatization development, follow the policy of active utilization, scientific development, law-based administration, and security assurance, advance the construction and interconnection of network infrastructure, encourage innovation in and application of network technologies, support the cultivation of cybersecurity professionals, establish and improve the cybersecurity assurance system, and strengthen cybersecurity protection capabilities.

title: National cybersecurity strategy
summary: Editorial overview of Article 5: national cybersecurity strategy. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 国家制定并不断完善网络安全战略，明确保障网络安全的基本要求和主要目标，提出重点领域的网络安全政策、工作任务和措施。
fullText: 国家制定并不断完善网络安全战略，明确保障网络安全的基本要求和主要目标，提出重点领域的网络安全政策、工作任务和措施。

en.title: National cybersecurity strategy
paragraphs: The State shall formulate and continually improve its cybersecurity strategy, specify the basic requirements and principal objectives for safeguarding cybersecurity, and set out cybersecurity policies, work tasks, and measures for key sectors.
en.fullText: The State shall formulate and continually improve its cybersecurity strategy, specify the basic requirements and principal objectives for safeguarding cybersecurity, and set out cybersecurity policies, work tasks, and measures for key sectors.

title: National risk and threat response
summary: Editorial overview of Article 6: national risk and threat response. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 国家采取措施，监测、防御、处置来源于中华人民共和国境内外的网络安全风险和威胁，保护关键信息基础设施免受攻击、侵入、干扰和破坏，依法惩治网络违法犯罪活动，维护网络空间安全和秩序。
fullText: 国家采取措施，监测、防御、处置来源于中华人民共和国境内外的网络安全风险和威胁，保护关键信息基础设施免受攻击、侵入、干扰和破坏，依法惩治网络违法犯罪活动，维护网络空间安全和秩序。

en.title: National risk and threat response
paragraphs: The State shall take measures to monitor, defend against, and respond to cybersecurity risks and threats originating both within and outside the territory of the People's Republic of China; protect critical information infrastructure from attacks, intrusions, interference, and destruction; punish unlawful and criminal online activities in accordance with law; and preserve cyberspace security and order.
en.fullText: The State shall take measures to monitor, defend against, and respond to cybersecurity risks and threats originating both within and outside the territory of the People's Republic of China; protect critical information infrastructure from attacks, intrusions, interference, and destruction; punish unlawful and criminal online activities in accordance with law; and preserve cyberspace security and order.

title: Cybersecurity culture and public participation
summary: Editorial overview of Article 7: cybersecurity culture and public participation. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 国家倡导诚实守信、健康文明的网络行为，推动传播社会主义核心价值观，采取措施提高全社会的网络安全意识和水平，形成全社会共同参与促进网络安全的良好环境。
fullText: 国家倡导诚实守信、健康文明的网络行为，推动传播社会主义核心价值观，采取措施提高全社会的网络安全意识和水平，形成全社会共同参与促进网络安全的良好环境。

en.title: Cybersecurity culture and public participation
paragraphs: The State advocates honest, trustworthy, healthy, and civilized online conduct, promotes the dissemination of core socialist values, and takes measures to raise cybersecurity awareness and capabilities throughout society, so as to foster an environment in which all sectors of society participate in promoting cybersecurity.
en.fullText: The State advocates honest, trustworthy, healthy, and civilized online conduct, promotes the dissemination of core socialist values, and takes measures to raise cybersecurity awareness and capabilities throughout society, so as to foster an environment in which all sectors of society participate in promoting cybersecurity.

title: International cooperation on cyberspace governance
summary: Editorial overview of Article 8: international cooperation on cyberspace governance. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 国家积极开展网络空间治理、网络技术研发和标准制定、打击网络违法犯罪等方面的国际交流与合作，推动构建和平、安全、开放、合作的网络空间，建立多边、民主、透明的网络治理体系。
fullText: 国家积极开展网络空间治理、网络技术研发和标准制定、打击网络违法犯罪等方面的国际交流与合作，推动构建和平、安全、开放、合作的网络空间，建立多边、民主、透明的网络治理体系。

en.title: International cooperation on cyberspace governance
paragraphs: The State shall actively conduct international exchange and cooperation in such areas as cyberspace governance, research and development of network technologies, standard-setting, and combating cybercrime; promote a peaceful, secure, open, and cooperative cyberspace; and establish a multilateral, democratic, and transparent system of internet governance.
en.fullText: The State shall actively conduct international exchange and cooperation in such areas as cyberspace governance, research and development of network technologies, standard-setting, and combating cybercrime; promote a peaceful, secure, open, and cooperative cyberspace; and establish a multilateral, democratic, and transparent system of internet governance.

title: Regulatory responsibilities
summary: Editorial overview of Article 9: regulatory responsibilities. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 国家网信部门负责统筹协调网络安全工作和相关监督管理工作。国务院电信主管部门、公安部门和其他有关机关依照本法和有关法律、行政法规的规定，在各自职责范围内负责网络安全保护和监督管理工作。
县级以上地方人民政府有关部门的网络安全保护和监督管理职责，按照国家有关规定确定。
fullText:
国家网信部门负责统筹协调网络安全工作和相关监督管理工作。国务院电信主管部门、公安部门和其他有关机关依照本法和有关法律、行政法规的规定，在各自职责范围内负责网络安全保护和监督管理工作。

县级以上地方人民政府有关部门的网络安全保护和监督管理职责，按照国家有关规定确定。

en.title: Regulatory responsibilities
paragraphs: The national cyberspace administration authority is responsible for overall coordination of cybersecurity work and the related supervision and administration. The competent telecommunications department of the State Council, public security organs, and other relevant authorities are responsible, within their respective areas of responsibility and in accordance with this Law and relevant laws and administrative regulations, for cybersecurity protection, supervision, and administration.

The cybersecurity protection, supervision, and administration responsibilities of relevant departments of local people's governments at or above the county level shall be determined in accordance with relevant State provisions.
en.fullText:
The national cyberspace administration authority is responsible for overall coordination of cybersecurity work and the related supervision and administration. The competent telecommunications department of the State Council, public security organs, and other relevant authorities are responsible, within their respective areas of responsibility and in accordance with this Law and relevant laws and administrative regulations, for cybersecurity protection, supervision, and administration.

The cybersecurity protection, supervision, and administration responsibilities of relevant departments of local people's governments at or above the county level shall be determined in accordance with relevant State provisions.

title: Network-operator duties and social responsibility
summary: Editorial overview of Article 10: network-operator duties and social responsibility. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 网络运营者开展经营和服务活动，必须遵守法律、行政法规，尊重社会公德，遵守商业道德，诚实信用，履行网络安全保护义务，接受政府和社会的监督，承担社会责任。
fullText: 网络运营者开展经营和服务活动，必须遵守法律、行政法规，尊重社会公德，遵守商业道德，诚实信用，履行网络安全保护义务，接受政府和社会的监督，承担社会责任。

en.title: Network-operator duties and social responsibility
paragraphs: When conducting business and providing services, network operators must comply with laws and administrative regulations, respect social morality, observe business ethics, act in good faith, perform cybersecurity protection obligations, accept government and public supervision, and assume social responsibility.
en.fullText: When conducting business and providing services, network operators must comply with laws and administrative regulations, respect social morality, observe business ethics, act in good faith, perform cybersecurity protection obligations, accept government and public supervision, and assume social responsibility.

title: Security measures for networks and services
summary: Editorial overview of Article 11: security measures for networks and services. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 建设、运营网络或者通过网络提供服务，应当依照法律、行政法规的规定和国家标准的强制性要求，采取技术措施和其他必要措施，保障网络安全、稳定运行，有效应对网络安全事件，防范网络违法犯罪活动，维护网络数据的完整性、保密性和可用性。
fullText: 建设、运营网络或者通过网络提供服务，应当依照法律、行政法规的规定和国家标准的强制性要求，采取技术措施和其他必要措施，保障网络安全、稳定运行，有效应对网络安全事件，防范网络违法犯罪活动，维护网络数据的完整性、保密性和可用性。

en.title: Security measures for networks and services
paragraphs: Those that construct or operate networks or provide services through networks shall, in accordance with laws and administrative regulations and the mandatory requirements of national standards, adopt technical and other necessary measures to safeguard the secure and stable operation of networks, respond effectively to cybersecurity incidents, prevent unlawful and criminal online activities, and preserve the integrity, confidentiality, and availability of network data.
en.fullText: Those that construct or operate networks or provide services through networks shall, in accordance with laws and administrative regulations and the mandatory requirements of national standards, adopt technical and other necessary measures to safeguard the secure and stable operation of networks, respond effectively to cybersecurity incidents, prevent unlawful and criminal online activities, and preserve the integrity, confidentiality, and availability of network data.

title: Industry self-regulation
summary: Editorial overview of Article 12: industry self-regulation. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 网络相关行业组织按照章程，加强行业自律，制定网络安全行为规范，指导会员加强网络安全保护，提高网络安全保护水平，促进行业健康发展。
fullText: 网络相关行业组织按照章程，加强行业自律，制定网络安全行为规范，指导会员加强网络安全保护，提高网络安全保护水平，促进行业健康发展。

en.title: Industry self-regulation
paragraphs: Network-related industry organizations shall, in accordance with their charters, strengthen industry self-regulation, formulate codes of cybersecurity conduct, guide their members in strengthening cybersecurity protection, improve protection levels, and promote the sound development of the industry.
en.fullText: Network-related industry organizations shall, in accordance with their charters, strengthen industry self-regulation, formulate codes of cybersecurity conduct, guide their members in strengthening cybersecurity protection, improve protection levels, and promote the sound development of the industry.

title: Lawful network use and prohibited conduct
summary: Editorial overview of Article 13: lawful network use and prohibited conduct. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 国家保护公民、法人和其他组织依法使用网络的权利，促进网络接入普及，提升网络服务水平，为社会提供安全、便利的网络服务，保障网络信息依法有序自由流动。
任何个人和组织使用网络应当遵守宪法法律，遵守公共秩序，尊重社会公德，不得危害网络安全，不得利用网络从事危害国家安全、荣誉和利益，煽动颠覆国家政权、推翻社会主义制度，煽动分裂国家、破坏国家统一，宣扬恐怖主义、极端主义，宣扬民族仇恨、民族歧视，传播暴力、淫秽色情信息，编造、传播虚假信息扰乱经济秩序和社会秩序，以及侵害他人名誉、隐私、知识产权和其他合法权益等活动。
fullText:
国家保护公民、法人和其他组织依法使用网络的权利，促进网络接入普及，提升网络服务水平，为社会提供安全、便利的网络服务，保障网络信息依法有序自由流动。

任何个人和组织使用网络应当遵守宪法法律，遵守公共秩序，尊重社会公德，不得危害网络安全，不得利用网络从事危害国家安全、荣誉和利益，煽动颠覆国家政权、推翻社会主义制度，煽动分裂国家、破坏国家统一，宣扬恐怖主义、极端主义，宣扬民族仇恨、民族歧视，传播暴力、淫秽色情信息，编造、传播虚假信息扰乱经济秩序和社会秩序，以及侵害他人名誉、隐私、知识产权和其他合法权益等活动。

en.title: Lawful network use and prohibited conduct
paragraphs: The State protects the rights of citizens, legal persons, and other organizations to use networks in accordance with law; promotes universal network access; raises the level of network services; provides secure and convenient network services to society; and safeguards the lawful, orderly, and free flow of network information.

Every individual and organization using networks shall comply with the Constitution and laws, observe public order, and respect social morality; shall not endanger cybersecurity; and shall not use networks to engage in activities that endanger national security, honor, or interests; incite subversion of State power or overthrow of the socialist system; incite separatism or undermine national unity; advocate terrorism or extremism; incite ethnic hatred or discrimination; disseminate violent or pornographic information; fabricate or disseminate false information that disrupts economic or social order; or infringe another person's reputation, privacy, intellectual property, or other lawful rights and interests.
en.fullText:
The State protects the rights of citizens, legal persons, and other organizations to use networks in accordance with law; promotes universal network access; raises the level of network services; provides secure and convenient network services to society; and safeguards the lawful, orderly, and free flow of network information.

Every individual and organization using networks shall comply with the Constitution and laws, observe public order, and respect social morality; shall not endanger cybersecurity; and shall not use networks to engage in activities that endanger national security, honor, or interests; incite subversion of State power or overthrow of the socialist system; incite separatism or undermine national unity; advocate terrorism or extremism; incite ethnic hatred or discrimination; disseminate violent or pornographic information; fabricate or disseminate false information that disrupts economic or social order; or infringe another person's reputation, privacy, intellectual property, or other lawful rights and interests.

title: Protection of minors online
summary: Editorial overview of Article 14: protection of minors online. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 国家支持研究开发有利于未成年人健康成长的网络产品和服务，依法惩治利用网络从事危害未成年人身心健康的活动，为未成年人提供安全、健康的网络环境。
fullText: 国家支持研究开发有利于未成年人健康成长的网络产品和服务，依法惩治利用网络从事危害未成年人身心健康的活动，为未成年人提供安全、健康的网络环境。

en.title: Protection of minors online
paragraphs: The State supports the research and development of network products and services conducive to the healthy development of minors, punishes in accordance with law activities that use networks to harm minors' physical or mental health, and provides minors with a safe and healthy online environment.
en.fullText: The State supports the research and development of network products and services conducive to the healthy development of minors, punishes in accordance with law activities that use networks to harm minors' physical or mental health, and provides minors with a safe and healthy online environment.

title: Reporting cybersecurity threats
summary: Editorial overview of Article 15: reporting cybersecurity threats. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 任何个人和组织有权对危害网络安全的行为向网信、电信、公安等部门举报。收到举报的部门应当及时依法作出处理；不属于本部门职责的，应当及时移送有权处理的部门。
有关部门应当对举报人的相关信息予以保密，保护举报人的合法权益。
fullText:
任何个人和组织有权对危害网络安全的行为向网信、电信、公安等部门举报。收到举报的部门应当及时依法作出处理；不属于本部门职责的，应当及时移送有权处理的部门。

有关部门应当对举报人的相关信息予以保密，保护举报人的合法权益。

en.title: Reporting cybersecurity threats
paragraphs: Every individual and organization has the right to report conduct that endangers cybersecurity to cyberspace administration, telecommunications, public security, and other departments. A department receiving a report shall promptly handle it in accordance with law; if the matter does not fall within its responsibilities, it shall promptly transfer the matter to a department with authority to handle it.
Relevant departments shall keep information concerning the reporting person confidential and protect that person's lawful rights and interests.
en.fullText:
Every individual and organization has the right to report conduct that endangers cybersecurity to cyberspace administration, telecommunications, public security, and other departments. A department receiving a report shall promptly handle it in accordance with law; if the matter does not fall within its responsibilities, it shall promptly transfer the matter to a department with authority to handle it.

Relevant departments shall keep information concerning the reporting person confidential and protect that person's lawful rights and interests.

title: Cybersecurity standards system
summary: Editorial overview of Article 16: cybersecurity standards system. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 国家建立和完善网络安全标准体系。国务院标准化行政主管部门和国务院其他有关部门根据各自的职责，组织制定并适时修订有关网络安全管理以及网络产品、服务和运行安全的国家标准、行业标准。
国家支持企业、研究机构、高等学校、网络相关行业组织参与网络安全国家标准、行业标准的制定。
fullText:
国家建立和完善网络安全标准体系。国务院标准化行政主管部门和国务院其他有关部门根据各自的职责，组织制定并适时修订有关网络安全管理以及网络产品、服务和运行安全的国家标准、行业标准。

国家支持企业、研究机构、高等学校、网络相关行业组织参与网络安全国家标准、行业标准的制定。

en.title: Cybersecurity standards system
paragraphs: The State shall establish and improve the cybersecurity standards system. The competent standardization department of the State Council and other departments of the State Council shall, according to their respective responsibilities, organize the formulation and timely revision of national and industry standards concerning cybersecurity management and the security of network products, services, and operations.

The State supports enterprises, research institutions, institutions of higher education, and network-related industry organizations in participating in the formulation of national and industry cybersecurity standards.
en.fullText:
The State shall establish and improve the cybersecurity standards system. The competent standardization department of the State Council and other departments of the State Council shall, according to their respective responsibilities, organize the formulation and timely revision of national and industry standards concerning cybersecurity management and the security of network products, services, and operations.

The State supports enterprises, research institutions, institutions of higher education, and network-related industry organizations in participating in the formulation of national and industry cybersecurity standards.

title: Support for cybersecurity technology and industry
summary: Editorial overview of Article 17: support for cybersecurity technology and industry. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 国务院和省、自治区、直辖市人民政府应当统筹规划，加大投入，扶持重点网络安全技术产业和项目，支持网络安全技术的研究开发和应用，推广安全可信的网络产品和服务，保护网络技术知识产权，支持企业、研究机构和高等学校等参与国家网络安全技术创新项目。
fullText: 国务院和省、自治区、直辖市人民政府应当统筹规划，加大投入，扶持重点网络安全技术产业和项目，支持网络安全技术的研究开发和应用，推广安全可信的网络产品和服务，保护网络技术知识产权，支持企业、研究机构和高等学校等参与国家网络安全技术创新项目。

en.title: Support for cybersecurity technology and industry
paragraphs: The State Council and the people's governments of provinces, autonomous regions, and municipalities directly under the Central Government shall make overall plans, increase investment, support key cybersecurity technology industries and projects, support the research, development, and application of cybersecurity technologies, promote secure and trustworthy network products and services, protect intellectual property rights in network technologies, and support enterprises, research institutions, and institutions of higher education in participating in national cybersecurity technology innovation projects.
en.fullText: The State Council and the people's governments of provinces, autonomous regions, and municipalities directly under the Central Government shall make overall plans, increase investment, support key cybersecurity technology industries and projects, support the research, development, and application of cybersecurity technologies, promote secure and trustworthy network products and services, protect intellectual property rights in network technologies, and support enterprises, research institutions, and institutions of higher education in participating in national cybersecurity technology innovation projects.

en.title: Cybersecurity certification, testing, and assessment services
paragraphs: The State shall advance the development of a market-based cybersecurity service system and encourage relevant enterprises and institutions to provide security services such as cybersecurity certification, testing, and risk assessment.
en.fullText: The State shall advance the development of a market-based cybersecurity service system and encourage relevant enterprises and institutions to provide security services such as cybersecurity certification, testing, and risk assessment.

fullText: 国家鼓励开发网络数据安全保护和利用技术，促进公共数据资源开放，推动技术创新和经济社会发展。

en.title: Network-data protection and utilization technologies
paragraphs: The State encourages the development of technologies for protecting and using network data, promotes the opening of public data resources, and advances technological innovation and economic and social development.

title: Artificial-intelligence security and development
summary: Editorial overview of Article 20: artificial-intelligence security and development. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 国家支持人工智能基础理论研究和算法等关键技术研发，推进训练数据资源、算力等基础设施建设，完善人工智能伦理规范，加强风险监测评估和安全监管，促进人工智能应用和健康发展。
国家支持创新网络安全管理方式，运用人工智能等新技术，提升网络安全保护水平。
fullText:
国家支持人工智能基础理论研究和算法等关键技术研发，推进训练数据资源、算力等基础设施建设，完善人工智能伦理规范，加强风险监测评估和安全监管，促进人工智能应用和健康发展。

国家支持创新网络安全管理方式，运用人工智能等新技术，提升网络安全保护水平。

en.title: Artificial-intelligence security and development
paragraphs: The State supports basic theoretical research in artificial intelligence and the research and development of key technologies such as algorithms; advances infrastructure development for training-data resources and computing power; improves artificial-intelligence ethics standards; strengthens risk monitoring, assessment, and security supervision; and promotes the application and sound development of artificial intelligence.
The State supports innovation in cybersecurity management methods and the use of artificial intelligence and other new technologies to improve the level of cybersecurity protection.
en.fullText:
The State supports basic theoretical research in artificial intelligence and the research and development of key technologies such as algorithms; advances infrastructure development for training-data resources and computing power; improves artificial-intelligence ethics standards; strengthens risk monitoring, assessment, and security supervision; and promotes the application and sound development of artificial intelligence.

The State supports innovation in cybersecurity management methods and the use of artificial intelligence and other new technologies to improve the level of cybersecurity protection.

title: Public cybersecurity education
summary: Editorial overview of Article 21: public cybersecurity education. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 各级人民政府及其有关部门应当组织开展经常性的网络安全宣传教育，并指导、督促有关单位做好网络安全宣传教育工作。
大众传播媒介应当有针对性地面向社会进行网络安全宣传教育。
fullText:
各级人民政府及其有关部门应当组织开展经常性的网络安全宣传教育，并指导、督促有关单位做好网络安全宣传教育工作。

大众传播媒介应当有针对性地面向社会进行网络安全宣传教育。

en.title: Public cybersecurity education
paragraphs: People's governments at all levels and their relevant departments shall regularly organize cybersecurity publicity and education and shall guide and supervise relevant entities in conducting cybersecurity publicity and education.
Mass media shall conduct targeted cybersecurity publicity and education for the public.
en.fullText:
People's governments at all levels and their relevant departments shall regularly organize cybersecurity publicity and education and shall guide and supervise relevant entities in conducting cybersecurity publicity and education.

Mass media shall conduct targeted cybersecurity publicity and education for the public.

title: Cybersecurity education and workforce development
summary: Editorial overview of Article 22: cybersecurity education and workforce development. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 国家支持企业和高等学校、职业学校等教育培训机构开展网络安全相关教育与培训，采取多种方式培养网络安全人才，促进网络安全人才交流。
fullText: 国家支持企业和高等学校、职业学校等教育培训机构开展网络安全相关教育与培训，采取多种方式培养网络安全人才，促进网络安全人才交流。

en.title: Cybersecurity education and workforce development
paragraphs: The State supports enterprises, institutions of higher education, vocational schools, and other education and training institutions in providing cybersecurity-related education and training, cultivating cybersecurity professionals through diverse means, and promoting exchanges among cybersecurity professionals.
en.fullText: The State supports enterprises, institutions of higher education, vocational schools, and other education and training institutions in providing cybersecurity-related education and training, cultivating cybersecurity professionals through diverse means, and promoting exchanges among cybersecurity professionals.

title: Classified cybersecurity protection duties
summary: Editorial overview of Article 23: classified cybersecurity protection duties. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 国家实行网络安全等级保护制度。网络运营者应当按照网络安全等级保护制度的要求，履行下列安全保护义务，保障网络免受干扰、破坏或者未经授权的访问，防止网络数据泄露或者被窃取、篡改：
（一）制定内部安全管理制度和操作规程，确定网络安全负责人，落实网络安全保护责任；
（二）采取防范计算机病毒和网络攻击、网络侵入等危害网络安全行为的技术措施；
（三）采取监测、记录网络运行状态、网络安全事件的技术措施，并按照规定留存相关的网络日志不少于六个月；
（四）采取数据分类、重要数据备份和加密等措施；
（五）法律、行政法规规定的其他义务。
fullText:
国家实行网络安全等级保护制度。网络运营者应当按照网络安全等级保护制度的要求，履行下列安全保护义务，保障网络免受干扰、破坏或者未经授权的访问，防止网络数据泄露或者被窃取、篡改：

（一）制定内部安全管理制度和操作规程，确定网络安全负责人，落实网络安全保护责任；

（二）采取防范计算机病毒和网络攻击、网络侵入等危害网络安全行为的技术措施；

（三）采取监测、记录网络运行状态、网络安全事件的技术措施，并按照规定留存相关的网络日志不少于六个月；

（四）采取数据分类、重要数据备份和加密等措施；

（五）法律、行政法规规定的其他义务。

en.title: Classified cybersecurity protection duties
paragraphs: The State implements a classified cybersecurity protection system. In accordance with the requirements of that system, network operators shall perform the following security-protection obligations to protect networks from interference, destruction, or unauthorized access and to prevent network data from being leaked, stolen, or altered:
(1) formulate internal security-management rules and operating procedures, designate a person responsible for cybersecurity, and implement cybersecurity responsibilities;
(2) adopt technical measures to prevent computer viruses, cyberattacks, network intrusions, and other conduct that endangers cybersecurity;

(3) adopt technical measures to monitor and record network operating conditions and cybersecurity incidents, and retain relevant network logs for no less than six months in accordance with relevant provisions;
(4) adopt measures such as data classification, backups of important data, and encryption; and
(5) perform other obligations prescribed by laws and administrative regulations.

title: Security obligations for network products and services
summary: Editorial overview of Article 24: security obligations for network products and services. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 网络产品、服务应当符合相关国家标准的强制性要求。网络产品、服务的提供者不得设置恶意程序；发现其网络产品、服务存在安全缺陷、漏洞等风险时，应当立即采取补救措施，按照规定及时告知用户并向有关主管部门报告。
网络产品、服务的提供者应当为其产品、服务持续提供安全维护；在规定或者当事人约定的期限内，不得终止提供安全维护。
网络产品、服务具有收集用户信息功能的，其提供者应当向用户明示并取得同意；涉及用户个人信息的，还应当遵守本法和有关法律、行政法规关于个人信息保护的规定。
fullText:
网络产品、服务应当符合相关国家标准的强制性要求。网络产品、服务的提供者不得设置恶意程序；发现其网络产品、服务存在安全缺陷、漏洞等风险时，应当立即采取补救措施，按照规定及时告知用户并向有关主管部门报告。

网络产品、服务的提供者应当为其产品、服务持续提供安全维护；在规定或者当事人约定的期限内，不得终止提供安全维护。

网络产品、服务具有收集用户信息功能的，其提供者应当向用户明示并取得同意；涉及用户个人信息的，还应当遵守本法和有关法律、行政法规关于个人信息保护的规定。

en.title: Security obligations for network products and services
paragraphs: Network products and services shall comply with the mandatory requirements of relevant national standards. Providers of network products and services shall not install malicious programs; when they discover security defects, vulnerabilities, or other risks in their products or services, they shall immediately take remedial measures and, in accordance with relevant provisions, promptly inform users and report to the relevant competent authorities.

Providers of network products and services shall provide continuous security maintenance for their products and services and shall not terminate security maintenance within the period prescribed by relevant provisions or agreed with the parties.

If a network product or service has a function that collects user information, its provider shall clearly inform users and obtain consent. If personal information is involved, the provider shall also comply with this Law and relevant laws and administrative regulations concerning personal-information protection.
en.fullText:
Network products and services shall comply with the mandatory requirements of relevant national standards. Providers of network products and services shall not install malicious programs; when they discover security defects, vulnerabilities, or other risks in their products or services, they shall immediately take remedial measures and, in accordance with relevant provisions, promptly inform users and report to the relevant competent authorities.

Providers of network products and services shall provide continuous security maintenance for their products and services and shall not terminate security maintenance within the period prescribed by relevant provisions or agreed with the parties.

If a network product or service has a function that collects user information, its provider shall clearly inform users and obtain consent. If personal information is involved, the provider shall also comply with this Law and relevant laws and administrative regulations concerning personal-information protection.

title: Certification of critical network equipment and specialized products
summary: Editorial overview of Article 25: certification of critical network equipment and specialized products. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 网络关键设备和网络安全专用产品应当按照相关国家标准的强制性要求，由具备资格的机构安全认证合格或者安全检测符合要求后，方可销售或者提供。国家网信部门会同国务院有关部门制定、公布网络关键设备和网络安全专用产品目录，并推动安全认证和安全检测结果互认，避免重复认证、检测。
fullText: 网络关键设备和网络安全专用产品应当按照相关国家标准的强制性要求，由具备资格的机构安全认证合格或者安全检测符合要求后，方可销售或者提供。国家网信部门会同国务院有关部门制定、公布网络关键设备和网络安全专用产品目录，并推动安全认证和安全检测结果互认，避免重复认证、检测。

en.title: Certification of critical network equipment and specialized products
paragraphs: Critical network equipment and specialized cybersecurity products may be sold or supplied only after they have passed security certification by a qualified institution or met security-testing requirements, in accordance with the mandatory requirements of relevant national standards. The national cyberspace administration authority, together with relevant departments of the State Council, shall formulate and publish a catalogue of critical network equipment and specialized cybersecurity products and shall promote mutual recognition of security-certification and security-testing results to avoid duplicative certification and testing.
en.fullText: Critical network equipment and specialized cybersecurity products may be sold or supplied only after they have passed security certification by a qualified institution or met security-testing requirements, in accordance with the mandatory requirements of relevant national standards. The national cyberspace administration authority, together with relevant departments of the State Council, shall formulate and publish a catalogue of critical network equipment and specialized cybersecurity products and shall promote mutual recognition of security-certification and security-testing results to avoid duplicative certification and testing.

title: Real-name information and trusted digital identity
summary: Editorial overview of Article 26: real-name information and trusted digital identity. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 网络运营者为用户办理网络接入、域名注册服务，办理固定电话、移动电话等入网手续，或者为用户提供信息发布、即时通讯等服务，在与用户签订协议或者确认提供服务时，应当要求用户提供真实身份信息。用户不提供真实身份信息的，网络运营者不得为其提供相关服务。
国家实施网络可信身份战略，支持研究开发安全、方便的电子身份认证技术，推动不同电子身份认证之间的互认。
fullText:
网络运营者为用户办理网络接入、域名注册服务，办理固定电话、移动电话等入网手续，或者为用户提供信息发布、即时通讯等服务，在与用户签订协议或者确认提供服务时，应当要求用户提供真实身份信息。用户不提供真实身份信息的，网络运营者不得为其提供相关服务。

国家实施网络可信身份战略，支持研究开发安全、方便的电子身份认证技术，推动不同电子身份认证之间的互认。

en.title: Real-name information and trusted digital identity
paragraphs: When network operators provide users with network access or domain-name registration services, handle network-access procedures for fixed-line or mobile telephone services, or provide users with information-publication or instant-messaging services, they shall require users to provide authentic identity information when entering into an agreement with the user or confirming that services will be provided. If a user does not provide authentic identity information, the network operator shall not provide the relevant services.

The State implements a trusted online identity strategy, supports research and development of secure and convenient electronic identity-authentication technologies, and promotes mutual recognition among different forms of electronic identity authentication.
en.fullText:
When network operators provide users with network access or domain-name registration services, handle network-access procedures for fixed-line or mobile telephone services, or provide users with information-publication or instant-messaging services, they shall require users to provide authentic identity information when entering into an agreement with the user or confirming that services will be provided. If a user does not provide authentic identity information, the network operator shall not provide the relevant services.

The State implements a trusted online identity strategy, supports research and development of secure and convenient electronic identity-authentication technologies, and promotes mutual recognition among different forms of electronic identity authentication.

title: Cybersecurity incident-response plans
summary: Editorial overview of Article 27: cybersecurity incident-response plans. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 网络运营者应当制定网络安全事件应急预案，及时处置系统漏洞、计算机病毒、网络攻击、网络侵入等安全风险；在发生危害网络安全的事件时，立即启动应急预案，采取相应的补救措施，并按照规定向有关主管部门报告。
fullText: 网络运营者应当制定网络安全事件应急预案，及时处置系统漏洞、计算机病毒、网络攻击、网络侵入等安全风险；在发生危害网络安全的事件时，立即启动应急预案，采取相应的补救措施，并按照规定向有关主管部门报告。

en.title: Cybersecurity incident-response plans
paragraphs: Network operators shall formulate cybersecurity incident-response plans and promptly address security risks such as system vulnerabilities, computer viruses, cyberattacks, and network intrusions. When an incident endangering cybersecurity occurs, they shall immediately activate the response plan, take corresponding remedial measures, and report to the relevant competent authorities in accordance with relevant provisions.
en.fullText: Network operators shall formulate cybersecurity incident-response plans and promptly address security risks such as system vulnerabilities, computer viruses, cyberattacks, and network intrusions. When an incident endangering cybersecurity occurs, they shall immediately activate the response plan, take corresponding remedial measures, and report to the relevant competent authorities in accordance with relevant provisions.

title: Rules for security testing and vulnerability disclosure
summary: Editorial overview of Article 28: rules for security testing and vulnerability disclosure. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 开展网络安全认证、检测、风险评估等活动，向社会发布系统漏洞、计算机病毒、网络攻击、网络侵入等网络安全信息，应当遵守国家有关规定。
fullText: 开展网络安全认证、检测、风险评估等活动，向社会发布系统漏洞、计算机病毒、网络攻击、网络侵入等网络安全信息，应当遵守国家有关规定。

en.title: Rules for security testing and vulnerability disclosure
paragraphs: Cybersecurity certification, testing, and risk-assessment activities, as well as the public release of cybersecurity information concerning system vulnerabilities, computer viruses, cyberattacks, and network intrusions, shall comply with relevant State provisions.
en.fullText: Cybersecurity certification, testing, and risk-assessment activities, as well as the public release of cybersecurity information concerning system vulnerabilities, computer viruses, cyberattacks, and network intrusions, shall comply with relevant State provisions.

title: Prohibited cybersecurity attacks and assistance
summary: Editorial overview of Article 29: prohibited cybersecurity attacks and assistance. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 任何个人和组织不得从事非法侵入他人网络、干扰他人网络正常功能、窃取网络数据等危害网络安全的活动；不得提供专门用于从事侵入网络、干扰网络正常功能及防护措施、窃取网络数据等危害网络安全活动的程序、工具；明知他人从事危害网络安全的活动的，不得为其提供技术支持、广告推广、支付结算等帮助。
fullText: 任何个人和组织不得从事非法侵入他人网络、干扰他人网络正常功能、窃取网络数据等危害网络安全的活动；不得提供专门用于从事侵入网络、干扰网络正常功能及防护措施、窃取网络数据等危害网络安全活动的程序、工具；明知他人从事危害网络安全的活动的，不得为其提供技术支持、广告推广、支付结算等帮助。

en.title: Prohibited cybersecurity attacks and assistance
paragraphs: No individual or organization may engage in activities that endanger cybersecurity, such as unlawfully intruding into another person's network, interfering with the normal functions of another person's network, or stealing network data; provide programs or tools specifically used to intrude into networks, interfere with normal network functions or protective measures, steal network data, or otherwise endanger cybersecurity; or, while knowing that another person is engaging in activities that endanger cybersecurity, provide technical support, advertising or promotion, payment settlement, or other assistance for those activities.
en.fullText: No individual or organization may engage in activities that endanger cybersecurity, such as unlawfully intruding into another person's network, interfering with the normal functions of another person's network, or stealing network data; provide programs or tools specifically used to intrude into networks, interfere with normal network functions or protective measures, steal network data, or otherwise endanger cybersecurity; or, while knowing that another person is engaging in activities that endanger cybersecurity, provide technical support, advertising or promotion, payment settlement, or other assistance for those activities.

title: Technical assistance to security authorities
summary: Editorial overview of Article 30: technical assistance to security authorities. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 网络运营者应当为公安机关、国家安全机关依法维护国家安全和侦查犯罪的活动提供技术支持和协助。

en.title: Technical assistance to security authorities
paragraphs: Network operators shall provide technical support and assistance to public security organs and state security organs when those organs lawfully safeguard national security and investigate crimes.
en.fullText: Network operators shall provide technical support and assistance to public security organs and state security organs when those organs lawfully safeguard national security and investigate crimes.

summary: Editorial overview of Article 31: cybersecurity information sharing and cooperation. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 国家支持网络运营者之间在网络安全信息收集、分析、通报和应急处置等方面进行合作，提高网络运营者的安全保障能力。
有关行业组织建立健全本行业的网络安全保护规范和协作机制，加强对网络安全风险的分析评估，定期向会员进行风险警示，支持、协助会员应对网络安全风险。
fullText:
国家支持网络运营者之间在网络安全信息收集、分析、通报和应急处置等方面进行合作，提高网络运营者的安全保障能力。

有关行业组织建立健全本行业的网络安全保护规范和协作机制，加强对网络安全风险的分析评估，定期向会员进行风险警示，支持、协助会员应对网络安全风险。

en.title: Cybersecurity information sharing and cooperation
paragraphs: The State supports cooperation among network operators in collecting, analyzing, reporting, and sharing cybersecurity information and in emergency response, so as to improve network operators' security-assurance capabilities.

Relevant industry organizations shall establish and improve cybersecurity protection rules and cooperation mechanisms for their industries, strengthen analysis and assessment of cybersecurity risks, regularly issue risk alerts to their members, and support and assist members in responding to cybersecurity risks.
en.fullText:
The State supports cooperation among network operators in collecting, analyzing, reporting, and sharing cybersecurity information and in emergency response, so as to improve network operators' security-assurance capabilities.

Relevant industry organizations shall establish and improve cybersecurity protection rules and cooperation mechanisms for their industries, strengthen analysis and assessment of cybersecurity risks, regularly issue risk alerts to their members, and support and assist members in responding to cybersecurity risks.

title: Purpose limitation for regulatory information
summary: Editorial overview of Article 32: purpose limitation for regulatory information. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 网信部门和有关部门在履行网络安全保护职责中获取的信息，只能用于维护网络安全的需要，不得用于其他用途。
fullText: 网信部门和有关部门在履行网络安全保护职责中获取的信息，只能用于维护网络安全的需要，不得用于其他用途。

en.title: Purpose limitation for regulatory information
paragraphs: Information obtained by cyberspace administration and other relevant departments in performing cybersecurity-protection responsibilities may be used only as necessary to safeguard cybersecurity and shall not be used for any other purpose.
en.fullText: Information obtained by cyberspace administration and other relevant departments in performing cybersecurity-protection responsibilities may be used only as necessary to safeguard cybersecurity and shall not be used for any other purpose.

title: Scope and protection of critical information infrastructure
summary: Editorial overview of Article 33: scope and protection of critical information infrastructure. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 国家对公共通信和信息服务、能源、交通、水利、金融、公共服务、电子政务等重要行业和领域，以及其他一旦遭到破坏、丧失功能或者数据泄露，可能严重危害国家安全、国计民生、公共利益的关键信息基础设施，在网络安全等级保护制度的基础上，实行重点保护。关键信息基础设施的具体范围和安全保护办法由国务院制定。
国家鼓励关键信息基础设施以外的网络运营者自愿参与关键信息基础设施保护体系。
fullText:
国家对公共通信和信息服务、能源、交通、水利、金融、公共服务、电子政务等重要行业和领域，以及其他一旦遭到破坏、丧失功能或者数据泄露，可能严重危害国家安全、国计民生、公共利益的关键信息基础设施，在网络安全等级保护制度的基础上，实行重点保护。关键信息基础设施的具体范围和安全保护办法由国务院制定。

国家鼓励关键信息基础设施以外的网络运营者自愿参与关键信息基础设施保护体系。

en.title: Scope and protection of critical information infrastructure
paragraphs: The State shall provide priority protection, on the basis of the classified cybersecurity protection system, to critical information infrastructure in important industries and sectors such as public communications and information services, energy, transportation, water conservancy, finance, public services, and e-government, as well as other critical information infrastructure whose destruction, loss of function, or data leakage could seriously endanger national security, the national economy and people's livelihoods, or the public interest. The State Council shall prescribe the specific scope of critical information infrastructure and the measures for its security protection.
The State encourages network operators other than critical information infrastructure operators to participate voluntarily in the critical information infrastructure protection system.
en.fullText:
The State shall provide priority protection, on the basis of the classified cybersecurity protection system, to critical information infrastructure in important industries and sectors such as public communications and information services, energy, transportation, water conservancy, finance, public services, and e-government, as well as other critical information infrastructure whose destruction, loss of function, or data leakage could seriously endanger national security, the national economy and people's livelihoods, or the public interest. The State Council shall prescribe the specific scope of critical information infrastructure and the measures for its security protection.

The State encourages network operators other than critical information infrastructure operators to participate voluntarily in the critical information infrastructure protection system.

section.title: Operational Security of Critical Information Infrastructure

title: Critical-infrastructure protection planning and supervision
summary: Editorial overview of Article 34: critical-infrastructure protection planning and supervision. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 按照国务院规定的职责分工，负责关键信息基础设施安全保护工作的部门分别编制并组织实施本行业、本领域的关键信息基础设施安全规划，指导和监督关键信息基础设施运行安全保护工作。
fullText: 按照国务院规定的职责分工，负责关键信息基础设施安全保护工作的部门分别编制并组织实施本行业、本领域的关键信息基础设施安全规划，指导和监督关键信息基础设施运行安全保护工作。

en.title: Critical-infrastructure protection planning and supervision
paragraphs: In accordance with the division of responsibilities prescribed by the State Council, departments responsible for protecting the security of critical information infrastructure shall separately formulate and organize implementation of security plans for critical information infrastructure in their industries and sectors and shall guide and supervise the protection of its operational security.
en.fullText: In accordance with the division of responsibilities prescribed by the State Council, departments responsible for protecting the security of critical information infrastructure shall separately formulate and organize implementation of security plans for critical information infrastructure in their industries and sectors and shall guide and supervise the protection of its operational security.

section.label: Section 2
section.title: Operational Security of Critical Information Infrastructure

title: Security by design for critical information infrastructure
summary: Editorial overview of Article 35: security by design for critical information infrastructure. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 建设关键信息基础设施应当确保其具有支持业务稳定、持续运行的性能，并保证安全技术措施同步规划、同步建设、同步使用。
fullText: 建设关键信息基础设施应当确保其具有支持业务稳定、持续运行的性能，并保证安全技术措施同步规划、同步建设、同步使用。

en.title: Security by design for critical information infrastructure
paragraphs: Critical information infrastructure shall be constructed with capabilities that support stable and continuous business operations, and its security technical measures shall be planned, constructed, and put into use simultaneously with the infrastructure.
en.fullText: Critical information infrastructure shall be constructed with capabilities that support stable and continuous business operations, and its security technical measures shall be planned, constructed, and put into use simultaneously with the infrastructure.

section.label: Section 2
section.title: Operational Security of Critical Information Infrastructure

title: Additional duties of critical-infrastructure operators
summary: Editorial overview of Article 36: additional duties of critical-infrastructure operators. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 除本法第二十三条的规定外，关键信息基础设施的运营者还应当履行下列安全保护义务：
（一）设置专门安全管理机构和安全管理负责人，并对该负责人和关键岗位的人员进行安全背景审查；
（二）定期对从业人员进行网络安全教育、技术培训和技能考核；
（三）对重要系统和数据库进行容灾备份；
（四）制定网络安全事件应急预案，并定期进行演练；
（五）法律、行政法规规定的其他义务。
fullText:
除本法第二十三条的规定外，关键信息基础设施的运营者还应当履行下列安全保护义务：

（一）设置专门安全管理机构和安全管理负责人，并对该负责人和关键岗位的人员进行安全背景审查；

（二）定期对从业人员进行网络安全教育、技术培训和技能考核；

（三）对重要系统和数据库进行容灾备份；

（四）制定网络安全事件应急预案，并定期进行演练；

（五）法律、行政法规规定的其他义务。

en.title: Additional duties of critical-infrastructure operators
paragraphs: In addition to complying with Article 23 of this Law, critical information infrastructure operators shall perform the following security-protection obligations:
(1) establish a dedicated security-management body and designate a person responsible for security, and conduct security background checks on that person and personnel in critical positions;
(2) regularly provide employees with cybersecurity education, technical training, and skills assessments;
(3) implement disaster-recovery backups for important systems and databases;
(4) formulate cybersecurity incident-response plans and conduct regular drills; and
(5) perform other obligations prescribed by laws and administrative regulations.

section.title: Operational Security of Critical Information Infrastructure

title: National-security review of critical-infrastructure procurement
summary: Editorial overview of Article 37: national-security review of critical-infrastructure procurement. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 关键信息基础设施的运营者采购网络产品和服务，可能影响国家安全的，应当通过国家网信部门会同国务院有关部门组织的国家安全审查。
fullText: 关键信息基础设施的运营者采购网络产品和服务，可能影响国家安全的，应当通过国家网信部门会同国务院有关部门组织的国家安全审查。

en.title: National-security review of critical-infrastructure procurement
paragraphs: If the procurement of network products or services by a critical information infrastructure operator may affect national security, the procurement shall undergo a national-security review organized by the national cyberspace administration authority together with relevant departments of the State Council.
en.fullText: If the procurement of network products or services by a critical information infrastructure operator may affect national security, the procurement shall undergo a national-security review organized by the national cyberspace administration authority together with relevant departments of the State Council.

title: Security agreements for critical-infrastructure procurement
summary: Editorial overview of Article 38: security agreements for critical-infrastructure procurement. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 关键信息基础设施的运营者采购网络产品和服务，应当按照规定与提供者签订安全保密协议，明确安全和保密义务与责任。
fullText: 关键信息基础设施的运营者采购网络产品和服务，应当按照规定与提供者签订安全保密协议，明确安全和保密义务与责任。

en.title: Security agreements for critical-infrastructure procurement
paragraphs: When procuring network products and services, critical information infrastructure operators shall enter into security and confidentiality agreements with providers in accordance with relevant provisions, clearly setting out security and confidentiality obligations and responsibilities.
en.fullText: When procuring network products and services, critical information infrastructure operators shall enter into security and confidentiality agreements with providers in accordance with relevant provisions, clearly setting out security and confidentiality obligations and responsibilities.

section.label: Section 2
section.title: Operational Security of Critical Information Infrastructure

title: Data localization and outbound assessment for critical infrastructure
summary: Editorial overview of Article 39: data localization and outbound assessment for critical infrastructure. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 关键信息基础设施的运营者在中华人民共和国境内运营中收集和产生的个人信息和重要数据应当在境内存储。因业务需要，确需向境外提供的，应当按照国家网信部门会同国务院有关部门制定的办法进行安全评估；法律、行政法规另有规定的，依照其规定。
fullText: 关键信息基础设施的运营者在中华人民共和国境内运营中收集和产生的个人信息和重要数据应当在境内存储。因业务需要，确需向境外提供的，应当按照国家网信部门会同国务院有关部门制定的办法进行安全评估；法律、行政法规另有规定的，依照其规定。

en.title: Data localization and outbound assessment for critical infrastructure
paragraphs: Personal information and important data collected and generated by critical information infrastructure operators in the course of operations within the territory of the People's Republic of China shall be stored within the territory. If it is genuinely necessary to provide such information or data outside the territory for business needs, a security assessment shall be conducted in accordance with measures formulated by the national cyberspace administration authority together with relevant departments of the State Council; where laws or administrative regulations provide otherwise, those provisions apply.
en.fullText: Personal information and important data collected and generated by critical information infrastructure operators in the course of operations within the territory of the People's Republic of China shall be stored within the territory. If it is genuinely necessary to provide such information or data outside the territory for business needs, a security assessment shall be conducted in accordance with measures formulated by the national cyberspace administration authority together with relevant departments of the State Council; where laws or administrative regulations provide otherwise, those provisions apply.
