section.label: Section 2
section.title: Operational Security of Critical Information Infrastructure

title: Annual critical-infrastructure security assessment
summary: Editorial overview of Article 40: annual critical-infrastructure security assessment. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 关键信息基础设施的运营者应当自行或者委托网络安全服务机构对其网络的安全性和可能存在的风险每年至少进行一次检测评估，并将检测评估情况和改进措施报送相关负责关键信息基础设施安全保护工作的部门。
fullText: 关键信息基础设施的运营者应当自行或者委托网络安全服务机构对其网络的安全性和可能存在的风险每年至少进行一次检测评估，并将检测评估情况和改进措施报送相关负责关键信息基础设施安全保护工作的部门。

en.title: Annual critical-infrastructure security assessment
paragraphs: Critical information infrastructure operators shall, at least once each year, conduct themselves or entrust a cybersecurity service provider to conduct testing and assessment of their networks' security and potential risks, and shall submit the testing and assessment findings and improvement measures to the relevant departments responsible for protecting critical information infrastructure.
en.fullText: Critical information infrastructure operators shall, at least once each year, conduct themselves or entrust a cybersecurity service provider to conduct testing and assessment of their networks' security and potential risks, and shall submit the testing and assessment findings and improvement measures to the relevant departments responsible for protecting critical information infrastructure.

section.title: Operational Security of Critical Information Infrastructure

title: Coordinated critical-infrastructure protection measures
summary: Editorial overview of Article 41: coordinated critical-infrastructure protection measures. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 国家网信部门应当统筹协调有关部门对关键信息基础设施的安全保护采取下列措施：
（一）对关键信息基础设施的安全风险进行抽查检测，提出改进措施，必要时可以委托网络安全服务机构对网络存在的安全风险进行检测评估；
（二）定期组织关键信息基础设施的运营者进行网络安全应急演练，提高应对网络安全事件的水平和协同配合能力；
（三）促进有关部门、关键信息基础设施的运营者以及有关研究机构、网络安全服务机构等之间的网络安全信息共享；
（四）对网络安全事件的应急处置与网络功能的恢复等，提供技术支持和协助。
fullText:
国家网信部门应当统筹协调有关部门对关键信息基础设施的安全保护采取下列措施：

（一）对关键信息基础设施的安全风险进行抽查检测，提出改进措施，必要时可以委托网络安全服务机构对网络存在的安全风险进行检测评估；

（二）定期组织关键信息基础设施的运营者进行网络安全应急演练，提高应对网络安全事件的水平和协同配合能力；

（三）促进有关部门、关键信息基础设施的运营者以及有关研究机构、网络安全服务机构等之间的网络安全信息共享；

（四）对网络安全事件的应急处置与网络功能的恢复等，提供技术支持和协助。

en.title: Coordinated critical-infrastructure protection measures
paragraphs: The national cyberspace administration authority shall coordinate relevant departments in taking the following measures to protect critical information infrastructure:

(1) conduct spot checks and testing of security risks in critical information infrastructure and propose improvements, and, when necessary, entrust cybersecurity service providers to test and assess network security risks;

(2) regularly organize critical information infrastructure operators to conduct cybersecurity emergency drills, improving their ability to respond to cybersecurity incidents and coordinate their response;

(3) promote cybersecurity information sharing among relevant departments, critical information infrastructure operators, relevant research institutions, cybersecurity service providers, and other entities; and
(4) provide technical support and assistance for emergency handling of cybersecurity incidents and restoration of network functions.

section.title: Operational Security of Critical Information Infrastructure

title: User-information confidentiality and personal-information law
summary: Editorial overview of Article 42: user-information confidentiality and personal-information law. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 网络运营者应当对其收集的用户信息严格保密，并建立健全用户信息保护制度。
网络运营者处理个人信息，应当遵守本法和《中华人民共和国民法典》、《中华人民共和国个人信息保护法》等法律、行政法规的规定。
fullText:
网络运营者应当对其收集的用户信息严格保密，并建立健全用户信息保护制度。

网络运营者处理个人信息，应当遵守本法和《中华人民共和国民法典》、《中华人民共和国个人信息保护法》等法律、行政法规的规定。

en.title: User-information confidentiality and personal-information law
paragraphs: Network operators shall maintain strict confidentiality of user information they collect and shall establish and improve user-information protection systems.

When processing personal information, network operators shall comply with this Law, the Civil Code of the People's Republic of China, the Personal Information Protection Law of the People's Republic of China, and other laws and administrative regulations.
en.fullText:
Network operators shall maintain strict confidentiality of user information they collect and shall establish and improve user-information protection systems.

When processing personal information, network operators shall comply with this Law, the Civil Code of the People's Republic of China, the Personal Information Protection Law of the People's Republic of China, and other laws and administrative regulations.

title: Lawfulness, necessity, notice, and consent
summary: Editorial overview of Article 43: lawfulness, necessity, notice, and consent. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 网络运营者收集、使用个人信息，应当遵循合法、正当、必要的原则，公开收集、使用规则，明示收集、使用信息的目的、方式和范围，并经被收集者同意。
网络运营者不得收集与其提供的服务无关的个人信息，不得违反法律、行政法规的规定和双方的约定收集、使用个人信息，并应当依照法律、行政法规的规定和与用户的约定，处理其保存的个人信息。
fullText:
网络运营者收集、使用个人信息，应当遵循合法、正当、必要的原则，公开收集、使用规则，明示收集、使用信息的目的、方式和范围，并经被收集者同意。

网络运营者不得收集与其提供的服务无关的个人信息，不得违反法律、行政法规的规定和双方的约定收集、使用个人信息，并应当依照法律、行政法规的规定和与用户的约定，处理其保存的个人信息。

en.title: Lawfulness, necessity, notice, and consent
paragraphs: When collecting or using personal information, network operators shall follow the principles of lawfulness, propriety, and necessity; publish their rules for collection and use; clearly state the purposes, methods, and scope of collection and use; and obtain the consent of the person whose information is collected.

Network operators shall not collect personal information unrelated to the services they provide; shall not collect or use personal information in violation of laws, administrative regulations, or the parties' agreement; and shall process stored personal information in accordance with laws, administrative regulations, and their agreements with users.
en.fullText:
When collecting or using personal information, network operators shall follow the principles of lawfulness, propriety, and necessity; publish their rules for collection and use; clearly state the purposes, methods, and scope of collection and use; and obtain the consent of the person whose information is collected.

Network operators shall not collect personal information unrelated to the services they provide; shall not collect or use personal information in violation of laws, administrative regulations, or the parties' agreement; and shall process stored personal information in accordance with laws, administrative regulations, and their agreements with users.

title: Personal-information security and incident response
summary: Editorial overview of Article 44: personal-information security and incident response. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 网络运营者不得泄露、篡改、毁损其收集的个人信息；未经被收集者同意，不得向他人提供个人信息。但是，经过处理无法识别特定个人且不能复原的除外。
网络运营者应当采取技术措施和其他必要措施，确保其收集的个人信息安全，防止信息泄露、毁损、丢失。在发生或者可能发生个人信息泄露、毁损、丢失的情况时，应当立即采取补救措施，按照规定及时告知用户并向有关主管部门报告。
fullText:
网络运营者不得泄露、篡改、毁损其收集的个人信息；未经被收集者同意，不得向他人提供个人信息。但是，经过处理无法识别特定个人且不能复原的除外。

网络运营者应当采取技术措施和其他必要措施，确保其收集的个人信息安全，防止信息泄露、毁损、丢失。在发生或者可能发生个人信息泄露、毁损、丢失的情况时，应当立即采取补救措施，按照规定及时告知用户并向有关主管部门报告。

en.title: Personal-information security and incident response
paragraphs: Network operators shall not disclose, alter, or damage the personal information they collect and shall not provide it to another person without the consent of the person whose information was collected, except where the information has been processed so that it cannot identify a specific individual and cannot be restored.

Network operators shall adopt technical and other necessary measures to secure the personal information they collect and prevent it from being leaked, damaged, or lost. If personal information is or may be leaked, damaged, or lost, they shall immediately take remedial measures, promptly inform users in accordance with relevant provisions, and report to the relevant competent authorities.
en.fullText:
Network operators shall not disclose, alter, or damage the personal information they collect and shall not provide it to another person without the consent of the person whose information was collected, except where the information has been processed so that it cannot identify a specific individual and cannot be restored.

Network operators shall adopt technical and other necessary measures to secure the personal information they collect and prevent it from being leaked, damaged, or lost. If personal information is or may be leaked, damaged, or lost, they shall immediately take remedial measures, promptly inform users in accordance with relevant provisions, and report to the relevant competent authorities.

title: Deletion and correction rights
summary: Editorial overview of Article 45: deletion and correction rights. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 个人发现网络运营者违反法律、行政法规的规定或者双方的约定收集、使用其个人信息的，有权要求网络运营者删除其个人信息；发现网络运营者收集、存储的其个人信息有错误的，有权要求网络运营者予以更正。网络运营者应当采取措施予以删除或者更正。
fullText: 个人发现网络运营者违反法律、行政法规的规定或者双方的约定收集、使用其个人信息的，有权要求网络运营者删除其个人信息；发现网络运营者收集、存储的其个人信息有错误的，有权要求网络运营者予以更正。网络运营者应当采取措施予以删除或者更正。

en.title: Deletion and correction rights
paragraphs: If an individual discovers that a network operator has collected or used that individual's personal information in violation of laws, administrative regulations, or the parties' agreement, the individual has the right to require the network operator to delete the personal information. If an individual discovers an error in personal information collected or stored by a network operator, the individual has the right to require the network operator to correct it. The network operator shall take measures to delete or correct the information.
en.fullText: If an individual discovers that a network operator has collected or used that individual's personal information in violation of laws, administrative regulations, or the parties' agreement, the individual has the right to require the network operator to delete the personal information. If an individual discovers an error in personal information collected or stored by a network operator, the individual has the right to require the network operator to correct it. The network operator shall take measures to delete or correct the information.

title: Prohibition on unlawful personal-information acquisition and provision
summary: Editorial overview of Article 46: prohibition on unlawful personal-information acquisition and provision. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 任何个人和组织不得窃取或者以其他非法方式获取个人信息，不得非法出售或者非法向他人提供个人信息。
fullText: 任何个人和组织不得窃取或者以其他非法方式获取个人信息，不得非法出售或者非法向他人提供个人信息。

en.title: Prohibition on unlawful personal-information acquisition and provision
paragraphs: No individual or organization may steal or otherwise unlawfully obtain personal information, or unlawfully sell or provide personal information to another person.
en.fullText: No individual or organization may steal or otherwise unlawfully obtain personal information, or unlawfully sell or provide personal information to another person.

title: Confidentiality duties of supervisory authorities
summary: Editorial overview of Article 47: confidentiality duties of supervisory authorities. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 依法负有网络安全监督管理职责的部门及其工作人员，必须对在履行职责中知悉的个人信息、隐私和商业秘密严格保密，不得泄露、出售或者非法向他人提供。
fullText: 依法负有网络安全监督管理职责的部门及其工作人员，必须对在履行职责中知悉的个人信息、隐私和商业秘密严格保密，不得泄露、出售或者非法向他人提供。

en.title: Confidentiality duties of supervisory authorities
paragraphs: Departments lawfully responsible for cybersecurity supervision and administration, and their personnel, must maintain strict confidentiality of personal information, privacy, and trade secrets learned in the performance of their duties and shall not disclose, sell, or unlawfully provide such information to another person.
en.fullText: Departments lawfully responsible for cybersecurity supervision and administration, and their personnel, must maintain strict confidentiality of personal information, privacy, and trade secrets learned in the performance of their duties and shall not disclose, sell, or unlawfully provide such information to another person.

title: Prohibited websites, groups, and criminal information
summary: Editorial overview of Article 48: prohibited websites, groups, and criminal information. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 任何个人和组织应当对其使用网络的行为负责，不得设立用于实施诈骗，传授犯罪方法，制作或者销售违禁物品、管制物品等违法犯罪活动的网站、通讯群组，不得利用网络发布涉及实施诈骗，制作或者销售违禁物品、管制物品以及其他违法犯罪活动的信息。
fullText: 任何个人和组织应当对其使用网络的行为负责，不得设立用于实施诈骗，传授犯罪方法，制作或者销售违禁物品、管制物品等违法犯罪活动的网站、通讯群组，不得利用网络发布涉及实施诈骗，制作或者销售违禁物品、管制物品以及其他违法犯罪活动的信息。

en.title: Prohibited websites, groups, and criminal information
paragraphs: Every individual and organization is responsible for its use of networks and shall not establish websites or communication groups for committing fraud, teaching criminal methods, producing or selling prohibited or controlled items, or carrying out other unlawful or criminal activities; nor use networks to publish information concerning fraud, the production or sale of prohibited or controlled items, or other unlawful or criminal activities.
en.fullText: Every individual and organization is responsible for its use of networks and shall not establish websites or communication groups for committing fraud, teaching criminal methods, producing or selling prohibited or controlled items, or carrying out other unlawful or criminal activities; nor use networks to publish information concerning fraud, the production or sale of prohibited or controlled items, or other unlawful or criminal activities.

title: Network-operator content-management duties
summary: Editorial overview of Article 49: network-operator content-management duties. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 网络运营者应当加强对其用户发布的信息的管理，发现法律、行政法规禁止发布或者传输的信息的，应当立即停止传输该信息，采取消除等处置措施，防止信息扩散，保存有关记录，并向有关主管部门报告。
fullText: 网络运营者应当加强对其用户发布的信息的管理，发现法律、行政法规禁止发布或者传输的信息的，应当立即停止传输该信息，采取消除等处置措施，防止信息扩散，保存有关记录，并向有关主管部门报告。

en.title: Network-operator content-management duties
paragraphs: Network operators shall strengthen management of information published by their users. Upon discovering information whose publication or transmission is prohibited by laws or administrative regulations, a network operator shall immediately stop transmitting that information, take measures such as removal to prevent its spread, retain relevant records, and report to the relevant competent authorities.
en.fullText: Network operators shall strengthen management of information published by their users. Upon discovering information whose publication or transmission is prohibited by laws or administrative regulations, a network operator shall immediately stop transmitting that information, take measures such as removal to prevent its spread, retain relevant records, and report to the relevant competent authorities.

title: Malicious software and prohibited electronic information
summary: Editorial overview of Article 50: malicious software and prohibited electronic information. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 任何个人和组织发送的电子信息、提供的应用软件，不得设置恶意程序，不得含有法律、行政法规禁止发布或者传输的信息。
电子信息发送服务提供者和应用软件下载服务提供者，应当履行安全管理义务，知道其用户有前款规定行为的，应当停止提供服务，采取消除等处置措施，保存有关记录，并向有关主管部门报告。
fullText:
任何个人和组织发送的电子信息、提供的应用软件，不得设置恶意程序，不得含有法律、行政法规禁止发布或者传输的信息。

电子信息发送服务提供者和应用软件下载服务提供者，应当履行安全管理义务，知道其用户有前款规定行为的，应当停止提供服务，采取消除等处置措施，保存有关记录，并向有关主管部门报告。

en.title: Malicious software and prohibited electronic information
paragraphs: Electronic information sent, and application software supplied, by any individual or organization shall not contain malicious programs or information whose publication or transmission is prohibited by laws or administrative regulations.

Electronic-information delivery service providers and application-software download service providers shall perform security-management obligations. If they know that a user has engaged in conduct described in the preceding paragraph, they shall stop providing services, take measures such as removal, retain relevant records, and report to the relevant competent authorities.
en.fullText:
Electronic information sent, and application software supplied, by any individual or organization shall not contain malicious programs or information whose publication or transmission is prohibited by laws or administrative regulations.

Electronic-information delivery service providers and application-software download service providers shall perform security-management obligations. If they know that a user has engaged in conduct described in the preceding paragraph, they shall stop providing services, take measures such as removal, retain relevant records, and report to the relevant competent authorities.

title: Complaints, reports, and inspection cooperation
summary: Editorial overview of Article 51: complaints, reports, and inspection cooperation. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 网络运营者应当建立网络信息安全投诉、举报制度，公布投诉、举报方式等信息，及时受理并处理有关网络信息安全的投诉和举报。
网络运营者对网信部门和有关部门依法实施的监督检查，应当予以配合。
fullText:
网络运营者应当建立网络信息安全投诉、举报制度，公布投诉、举报方式等信息，及时受理并处理有关网络信息安全的投诉和举报。

网络运营者对网信部门和有关部门依法实施的监督检查，应当予以配合。

en.title: Complaints, reports, and inspection cooperation
paragraphs: Network operators shall establish systems for complaints and reports concerning network information security, publish information such as methods for submitting complaints and reports, and promptly accept and handle relevant complaints and reports.
Network operators shall cooperate with supervision and inspections lawfully conducted by cyberspace administration and other relevant departments.
en.fullText:
Network operators shall establish systems for complaints and reports concerning network information security, publish information such as methods for submitting complaints and reports, and promptly accept and handle relevant complaints and reports.

Network operators shall cooperate with supervision and inspections lawfully conducted by cyberspace administration and other relevant departments.

title: Regulatory handling of prohibited information
summary: Editorial overview of Article 52: regulatory handling of prohibited information. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 国家网信部门和有关部门依法履行网络信息安全监督管理职责，发现法律、行政法规禁止发布或者传输的信息的，应当要求网络运营者停止传输，采取消除等处置措施，保存有关记录；对来源于中华人民共和国境外的上述信息，应当通知有关机构采取技术措施和其他必要措施阻断传播。
fullText: 国家网信部门和有关部门依法履行网络信息安全监督管理职责，发现法律、行政法规禁止发布或者传输的信息的，应当要求网络运营者停止传输，采取消除等处置措施，保存有关记录；对来源于中华人民共和国境外的上述信息，应当通知有关机构采取技术措施和其他必要措施阻断传播。

en.title: Regulatory handling of prohibited information
paragraphs: When, in lawfully performing network-information-security supervision and administration duties, the national cyberspace administration authority or another relevant department discovers information whose publication or transmission is prohibited by laws or administrative regulations, it shall require the network operator to stop transmitting the information, take measures such as removal, and retain relevant records. For such information originating outside the territory of the People's Republic of China, it shall notify relevant institutions to take technical and other necessary measures to block its dissemination.
en.fullText: When, in lawfully performing network-information-security supervision and administration duties, the national cyberspace administration authority or another relevant department discovers information whose publication or transmission is prohibited by laws or administrative regulations, it shall require the network operator to stop transmitting the information, take measures such as removal, and retain relevant records. For such information originating outside the territory of the People's Republic of China, it shall notify relevant institutions to take technical and other necessary measures to block its dissemination.

title: National monitoring, early-warning, and information system

chapter.label: Chapter V
chapter.title: Monitoring, Early Warning, and Emergency Response

summary: Editorial overview of Article 53: national monitoring, early-warning, and information system. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 国家建立网络安全监测预警和信息通报制度。国家网信部门应当统筹协调有关部门加强网络安全信息收集、分析和通报工作，按照规定统一发布网络安全监测预警信息。
fullText: 国家建立网络安全监测预警和信息通报制度。国家网信部门应当统筹协调有关部门加强网络安全信息收集、分析和通报工作，按照规定统一发布网络安全监测预警信息。

en.title: National monitoring, early-warning, and information system
paragraphs: The State shall establish a cybersecurity monitoring, early-warning, and information-reporting system. The national cyberspace administration authority shall coordinate relevant departments in strengthening the collection, analysis, and reporting of cybersecurity information and shall uniformly issue cybersecurity monitoring and early-warning information in accordance with relevant provisions.
en.fullText: The State shall establish a cybersecurity monitoring, early-warning, and information-reporting system. The national cyberspace administration authority shall coordinate relevant departments in strengthening the collection, analysis, and reporting of cybersecurity information and shall uniformly issue cybersecurity monitoring and early-warning information in accordance with relevant provisions.

title: Sectoral critical-infrastructure monitoring and early warning

chapter.label: Chapter V
chapter.title: Monitoring, Early Warning, and Emergency Response

summary: Editorial overview of Article 54: sectoral critical-infrastructure monitoring and early warning. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 负责关键信息基础设施安全保护工作的部门，应当建立健全本行业、本领域的网络安全监测预警和信息通报制度，并按照规定报送网络安全监测预警信息。
fullText: 负责关键信息基础设施安全保护工作的部门，应当建立健全本行业、本领域的网络安全监测预警和信息通报制度，并按照规定报送网络安全监测预警信息。

en.title: Sectoral critical-infrastructure monitoring and early warning
paragraphs: Departments responsible for protecting critical information infrastructure shall establish and improve cybersecurity monitoring, early-warning, and information-reporting systems for their respective industries and sectors and shall submit cybersecurity monitoring and early-warning information in accordance with relevant provisions.
en.fullText: Departments responsible for protecting critical information infrastructure shall establish and improve cybersecurity monitoring, early-warning, and information-reporting systems for their respective industries and sectors and shall submit cybersecurity monitoring and early-warning information in accordance with relevant provisions.

title: Risk-assessment and emergency-response mechanisms

chapter.label: Chapter V
chapter.title: Monitoring, Early Warning, and Emergency Response

summary: Editorial overview of Article 55: risk-assessment and emergency-response mechanisms. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 国家网信部门协调有关部门建立健全网络安全风险评估和应急工作机制，制定网络安全事件应急预案，并定期组织演练。
负责关键信息基础设施安全保护工作的部门应当制定本行业、本领域的网络安全事件应急预案，并定期组织演练。
网络安全事件应急预案应当按照事件发生后的危害程度、影响范围等因素对网络安全事件进行分级，并规定相应的应急处置措施。
fullText:
国家网信部门协调有关部门建立健全网络安全风险评估和应急工作机制，制定网络安全事件应急预案，并定期组织演练。

负责关键信息基础设施安全保护工作的部门应当制定本行业、本领域的网络安全事件应急预案，并定期组织演练。

网络安全事件应急预案应当按照事件发生后的危害程度、影响范围等因素对网络安全事件进行分级，并规定相应的应急处置措施。

en.title: Risk-assessment and emergency-response mechanisms
paragraphs: The national cyberspace administration authority shall coordinate relevant departments in establishing and improving cybersecurity risk-assessment and emergency-response mechanisms, formulate cybersecurity incident-response plans, and regularly organize drills.

Departments responsible for protecting critical information infrastructure shall formulate cybersecurity incident-response plans for their respective industries and sectors and shall regularly organize drills.

Cybersecurity incident-response plans shall classify cybersecurity incidents according to factors such as the degree of harm and scope of impact following an incident and shall prescribe corresponding emergency-response measures.
en.fullText:
The national cyberspace administration authority shall coordinate relevant departments in establishing and improving cybersecurity risk-assessment and emergency-response mechanisms, formulate cybersecurity incident-response plans, and regularly organize drills.

Departments responsible for protecting critical information infrastructure shall formulate cybersecurity incident-response plans for their respective industries and sectors and shall regularly organize drills.

Cybersecurity incident-response plans shall classify cybersecurity incidents according to factors such as the degree of harm and scope of impact following an incident and shall prescribe corresponding emergency-response measures.

title: Measures when cybersecurity risk increases

chapter.label: Chapter V
chapter.title: Monitoring, Early Warning, and Emergency Response

summary: Editorial overview of Article 56: measures when cybersecurity risk increases. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 网络安全事件发生的风险增大时，省级以上人民政府有关部门应当按照规定的权限和程序，并根据网络安全风险的特点和可能造成的危害，采取下列措施：
（一）要求有关部门、机构和人员及时收集、报告有关信息，加强对网络安全风险的监测；
（二）组织有关部门、机构和专业人员，对网络安全风险信息进行分析评估，预测事件发生的可能性、影响范围和危害程度；
（三）向社会发布网络安全风险预警，发布避免、减轻危害的措施。
fullText:
网络安全事件发生的风险增大时，省级以上人民政府有关部门应当按照规定的权限和程序，并根据网络安全风险的特点和可能造成的危害，采取下列措施：

（一）要求有关部门、机构和人员及时收集、报告有关信息，加强对网络安全风险的监测；

（二）组织有关部门、机构和专业人员，对网络安全风险信息进行分析评估，预测事件发生的可能性、影响范围和危害程度；

（三）向社会发布网络安全风险预警，发布避免、减轻危害的措施。

en.title: Measures when cybersecurity risk increases
paragraphs: When the risk of a cybersecurity incident increases, relevant departments of people's governments at or above the provincial level shall, in accordance with prescribed authority and procedures and in light of the characteristics of the cybersecurity risk and the harm it may cause, take the following measures:
(1) require relevant departments, institutions, and personnel promptly to collect and report relevant information and strengthen cybersecurity-risk monitoring;

(2) organize relevant departments, institutions, and professionals to analyze and assess cybersecurity-risk information and predict the likelihood, scope of impact, and degree of harm of an incident; and
(3) issue cybersecurity-risk warnings to the public and publish measures for avoiding or mitigating harm.
en.fullText:
When the risk of a cybersecurity incident increases, relevant departments of people's governments at or above the provincial level shall, in accordance with prescribed authority and procedures and in light of the characteristics of the cybersecurity risk and the harm it may cause, take the following measures:

(1) require relevant departments, institutions, and personnel promptly to collect and report relevant information and strengthen cybersecurity-risk monitoring;

(2) organize relevant departments, institutions, and professionals to analyze and assess cybersecurity-risk information and predict the likelihood, scope of impact, and degree of harm of an incident; and

(3) issue cybersecurity-risk warnings to the public and publish measures for avoiding or mitigating harm.

title: Response to cybersecurity incidents

chapter.label: Chapter V
chapter.title: Monitoring, Early Warning, and Emergency Response

summary: Editorial overview of Article 57: response to cybersecurity incidents. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 发生网络安全事件，应当立即启动网络安全事件应急预案，对网络安全事件进行调查和评估，要求网络运营者采取技术措施和其他必要措施，消除安全隐患，防止危害扩大，并及时向社会发布与公众有关的警示信息。
fullText: 发生网络安全事件，应当立即启动网络安全事件应急预案，对网络安全事件进行调查和评估，要求网络运营者采取技术措施和其他必要措施，消除安全隐患，防止危害扩大，并及时向社会发布与公众有关的警示信息。

en.title: Response to cybersecurity incidents
paragraphs: When a cybersecurity incident occurs, the cybersecurity incident-response plan shall be activated immediately; the incident shall be investigated and assessed; network operators shall be required to take technical and other necessary measures to eliminate security hazards and prevent the harm from expanding; and warning information relevant to the public shall be issued promptly.
en.fullText: When a cybersecurity incident occurs, the cybersecurity incident-response plan shall be activated immediately; the incident shall be investigated and assessed; network operators shall be required to take technical and other necessary measures to eliminate security hazards and prevent the harm from expanding; and warning information relevant to the public shall be issued promptly.

title: Regulatory interviews and remediation

chapter.label: Chapter V
chapter.title: Monitoring, Early Warning, and Emergency Response

summary: Editorial overview of Article 58: regulatory interviews and remediation. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 省级以上人民政府有关部门在履行网络安全监督管理职责中，发现网络存在较大安全风险或者发生安全事件的，可以按照规定的权限和程序对该网络的运营者的法定代表人或者主要负责人进行约谈。网络运营者应当按照要求采取措施，进行整改，消除隐患。
fullText: 省级以上人民政府有关部门在履行网络安全监督管理职责中，发现网络存在较大安全风险或者发生安全事件的，可以按照规定的权限和程序对该网络的运营者的法定代表人或者主要负责人进行约谈。网络运营者应当按照要求采取措施，进行整改，消除隐患。

en.title: Regulatory interviews and remediation
paragraphs: When relevant departments of people's governments at or above the provincial level discover, in performing cybersecurity supervision and administration duties, that a network presents a relatively serious security risk or has experienced a security incident, they may, in accordance with prescribed authority and procedures, conduct a regulatory interview with the network operator's legal representative or principal person in charge. The network operator shall take measures as required, carry out rectification, and eliminate the hazard.
en.fullText: When relevant departments of people's governments at or above the provincial level discover, in performing cybersecurity supervision and administration duties, that a network presents a relatively serious security risk or has experienced a security incident, they may, in accordance with prescribed authority and procedures, conduct a regulatory interview with the network operator's legal representative or principal person in charge. The network operator shall take measures as required, carry out rectification, and eliminate the hazard.

title: Application of emergency and work-safety laws

chapter.label: Chapter V
chapter.title: Monitoring, Early Warning, and Emergency Response

summary: Editorial overview of Article 59: application of emergency and work-safety laws. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 因网络安全事件，发生突发事件或者生产安全事故的，应当依照《中华人民共和国突发事件应对法》、《中华人民共和国安全生产法》等有关法律、行政法规的规定处置。
fullText: 因网络安全事件，发生突发事件或者生产安全事故的，应当依照《中华人民共和国突发事件应对法》、《中华人民共和国安全生产法》等有关法律、行政法规的规定处置。

en.title: Application of emergency and work-safety laws
paragraphs: If a cybersecurity incident gives rise to an emergency or a work-safety accident, it shall be handled in accordance with the Emergency Response Law of the People's Republic of China, the Work Safety Law of the People's Republic of China, and other relevant laws and administrative regulations.
en.fullText: If a cybersecurity incident gives rise to an emergency or a work-safety accident, it shall be handled in accordance with the Emergency Response Law of the People's Republic of China, the Work Safety Law of the People's Republic of China, and other relevant laws and administrative regulations.

title: Temporary network-communication restrictions

chapter.label: Chapter V
chapter.title: Monitoring, Early Warning, and Emergency Response

summary: Editorial overview of Article 60: temporary network-communication restrictions. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 因维护国家安全和社会公共秩序，处置重大突发社会安全事件的需要，经国务院决定或者批准，可以在特定区域对网络通信采取限制等临时措施。
fullText: 因维护国家安全和社会公共秩序，处置重大突发社会安全事件的需要，经国务院决定或者批准，可以在特定区域对网络通信采取限制等临时措施。

en.title: Temporary network-communication restrictions
paragraphs: When necessary to safeguard national security and public order and to respond to a major public-security emergency, temporary measures such as restricting network communications within a specified area may be taken upon a decision or approval of the State Council.
en.fullText: When necessary to safeguard national security and public order and to respond to a major public-security emergency, temporary measures such as restricting network communications within a specified area may be taken upon a decision or approval of the State Council.

title: Penalties for failures in network and critical-infrastructure security
summary: Editorial overview of Article 61: penalties for failures in network and critical-infrastructure security. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 网络运营者不履行本法第二十三条、第二十七条规定的网络安全保护义务的，由有关主管部门责令改正，给予警告，可以处一万元以上五万元以下罚款；拒不改正或者导致危害网络安全等后果的，处五万元以上五十万元以下罚款，对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员处一万元以上十万元以下罚款。
关键信息基础设施的运营者不履行本法第三十五条、第三十六条、第三十八条、第四十条规定的网络安全保护义务的，由有关主管部门责令改正，给予警告，可以处五万元以上十万元以下罚款；拒不改正或者导致危害网络安全等后果的，处十万元以上一百万元以下罚款，对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员处一万元以上十万元以下罚款。
有前两款行为，造成大量数据泄露、关键信息基础设施丧失局部功能等严重危害网络安全后果的，由有关主管部门处五十万元以上二百万元以下罚款，对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员处五万元以上二十万元以下罚款；造成关键信息基础设施丧失主要功能等特别严重危害网络安全后果的，处二百万元以上一千万元以下罚款，对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员处二十万元以上一百万元以下罚款。
fullText:
网络运营者不履行本法第二十三条、第二十七条规定的网络安全保护义务的，由有关主管部门责令改正，给予警告，可以处一万元以上五万元以下罚款；拒不改正或者导致危害网络安全等后果的，处五万元以上五十万元以下罚款，对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员处一万元以上十万元以下罚款。

关键信息基础设施的运营者不履行本法第三十五条、第三十六条、第三十八条、第四十条规定的网络安全保护义务的，由有关主管部门责令改正，给予警告，可以处五万元以上十万元以下罚款；拒不改正或者导致危害网络安全等后果的，处十万元以上一百万元以下罚款，对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员处一万元以上十万元以下罚款。

有前两款行为，造成大量数据泄露、关键信息基础设施丧失局部功能等严重危害网络安全后果的，由有关主管部门处五十万元以上二百万元以下罚款，对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员处五万元以上二十万元以下罚款；造成关键信息基础设施丧失主要功能等特别严重危害网络安全后果的，处二百万元以上一千万元以下罚款，对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员处二十万元以上一百万元以下罚款。

en.title: Penalties for failures in network and critical-infrastructure security
paragraphs: If a network operator fails to perform the cybersecurity-protection obligations prescribed in Articles 23 and 27 of this Law, the relevant competent authority shall order correction and issue a warning and may impose a fine of not less than RMB 10,000 but not more than RMB 50,000. If the operator refuses to make corrections or causes consequences that endanger cybersecurity, a fine of not less than RMB 50,000 but not more than RMB 500,000 shall be imposed, and the directly responsible persons in charge and other directly responsible personnel shall each be fined not less than RMB 10,000 but not more than RMB 100,000.

If a critical information infrastructure operator fails to perform the cybersecurity-protection obligations prescribed in Articles 35, 36, 38, and 40 of this Law, the relevant competent authority shall order correction and issue a warning and may impose a fine of not less than RMB 50,000 but not more than RMB 100,000. If the operator refuses to make corrections or causes consequences that endanger cybersecurity, a fine of not less than RMB 100,000 but not more than RMB 1 million shall be imposed, and the directly responsible persons in charge and other directly responsible personnel shall each be fined not less than RMB 10,000 but not more than RMB 100,000.

If conduct described in either of the preceding two paragraphs causes serious cybersecurity consequences, such as a large-scale data leak or partial loss of function of critical information infrastructure, the relevant competent authority shall impose a fine of not less than RMB 500,000 but not more than RMB 2 million, and the directly responsible persons in charge and other directly responsible personnel shall each be fined not less than RMB 50,000 but not more than RMB 200,000. If it causes particularly serious cybersecurity consequences, such as loss of the principal functions of critical information infrastructure, a fine of not less than RMB 2 million but not more than RMB 10 million shall be imposed, and the directly responsible persons in charge and other directly responsible personnel shall each be fined not less than RMB 200,000 but not more than RMB 1 million.
en.fullText:
If a network operator fails to perform the cybersecurity-protection obligations prescribed in Articles 23 and 27 of this Law, the relevant competent authority shall order correction and issue a warning and may impose a fine of not less than RMB 10,000 but not more than RMB 50,000. If the operator refuses to make corrections or causes consequences that endanger cybersecurity, a fine of not less than RMB 50,000 but not more than RMB 500,000 shall be imposed, and the directly responsible persons in charge and other directly responsible personnel shall each be fined not less than RMB 10,000 but not more than RMB 100,000.

If a critical information infrastructure operator fails to perform the cybersecurity-protection obligations prescribed in Articles 35, 36, 38, and 40 of this Law, the relevant competent authority shall order correction and issue a warning and may impose a fine of not less than RMB 50,000 but not more than RMB 100,000. If the operator refuses to make corrections or causes consequences that endanger cybersecurity, a fine of not less than RMB 100,000 but not more than RMB 1 million shall be imposed, and the directly responsible persons in charge and other directly responsible personnel shall each be fined not less than RMB 10,000 but not more than RMB 100,000.

If conduct described in either of the preceding two paragraphs causes serious cybersecurity consequences, such as a large-scale data leak or partial loss of function of critical information infrastructure, the relevant competent authority shall impose a fine of not less than RMB 500,000 but not more than RMB 2 million, and the directly responsible persons in charge and other directly responsible personnel shall each be fined not less than RMB 50,000 but not more than RMB 200,000. If it causes particularly serious cybersecurity consequences, such as loss of the principal functions of critical information infrastructure, a fine of not less than RMB 2 million but not more than RMB 10 million shall be imposed, and the directly responsible persons in charge and other directly responsible personnel shall each be fined not less than RMB 200,000 but not more than RMB 1 million.

title: Penalties for unsafe products, services, and malicious programs
summary: Editorial overview of Article 62: penalties for unsafe products, services, and malicious programs. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 违反本法第二十四条第一款、第二款和第五十条第一款规定，有下列行为之一的，由有关主管部门责令改正，给予警告；拒不改正或者导致危害网络安全等后果的，处五万元以上五十万元以下罚款，对直接负责的主管人员处一万元以上十万元以下罚款：
（一）设置恶意程序的；
（二）对其产品、服务存在的安全缺陷、漏洞等风险未立即采取补救措施，或者未按照规定及时告知用户并向有关主管部门报告的；
（三）擅自终止为其产品、服务提供安全维护的。
有前款第一项、第二项行为，造成本法第六十一条第三款规定的后果的，依照该款规定处罚。
fullText:
违反本法第二十四条第一款、第二款和第五十条第一款规定，有下列行为之一的，由有关主管部门责令改正，给予警告；拒不改正或者导致危害网络安全等后果的，处五万元以上五十万元以下罚款，对直接负责的主管人员处一万元以上十万元以下罚款：

（一）设置恶意程序的；

（二）对其产品、服务存在的安全缺陷、漏洞等风险未立即采取补救措施，或者未按照规定及时告知用户并向有关主管部门报告的；

（三）擅自终止为其产品、服务提供安全维护的。

有前款第一项、第二项行为，造成本法第六十一条第三款规定的后果的，依照该款规定处罚。

en.title: Penalties for unsafe products, services, and malicious programs
paragraphs: If a person violates Article 24, paragraphs 1 or 2, or Article 50, paragraph 1, of this Law by engaging in any of the following conduct, the relevant competent authority shall order correction and issue a warning. If the person refuses to make corrections or causes consequences that endanger cybersecurity, a fine of not less than RMB 50,000 but not more than RMB 500,000 shall be imposed, and the directly responsible person in charge shall be fined not less than RMB 10,000 but not more than RMB 100,000:
(1) installing a malicious program;

(2) failing immediately to take remedial measures for security defects, vulnerabilities, or other risks in a product or service, or failing promptly to inform users and report to the relevant competent authorities in accordance with relevant provisions; or
(3) terminating security maintenance for a product or service without authorization.

If conduct described in item (1) or (2) of the preceding paragraph causes consequences prescribed in Article 61, paragraph 3, of this Law, penalties shall be imposed in accordance with that paragraph.
en.fullText:
If a person violates Article 24, paragraphs 1 or 2, or Article 50, paragraph 1, of this Law by engaging in any of the following conduct, the relevant competent authority shall order correction and issue a warning. If the person refuses to make corrections or causes consequences that endanger cybersecurity, a fine of not less than RMB 50,000 but not more than RMB 500,000 shall be imposed, and the directly responsible person in charge shall be fined not less than RMB 10,000 but not more than RMB 100,000:

(1) installing a malicious program;

(2) failing immediately to take remedial measures for security defects, vulnerabilities, or other risks in a product or service, or failing promptly to inform users and report to the relevant competent authorities in accordance with relevant provisions; or

(3) terminating security maintenance for a product or service without authorization.

If conduct described in item (1) or (2) of the preceding paragraph causes consequences prescribed in Article 61, paragraph 3, of this Law, penalties shall be imposed in accordance with that paragraph.

title: Penalties for uncertified critical equipment and products
summary: Editorial overview of Article 63: penalties for uncertified critical equipment and products. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 违反本法第二十五条规定，销售或者提供未经安全认证、安全检测或者安全认证不合格、安全检测不符合要求的网络关键设备和网络安全专用产品的，由有关主管部门责令停止销售或者提供，给予警告，没收违法所得；没有违法所得或者违法所得不足十万元的，并处二万元以上十万元以下罚款；违法所得十万元以上的，并处违法所得一倍以上五倍以下罚款；情节严重的，并可以责令暂停相关业务、停业整顿、吊销相关业务许可证或者吊销营业执照。法律、行政法规另有规定的，依照其规定。
fullText: 违反本法第二十五条规定，销售或者提供未经安全认证、安全检测或者安全认证不合格、安全检测不符合要求的网络关键设备和网络安全专用产品的，由有关主管部门责令停止销售或者提供，给予警告，没收违法所得；没有违法所得或者违法所得不足十万元的，并处二万元以上十万元以下罚款；违法所得十万元以上的，并处违法所得一倍以上五倍以下罚款；情节严重的，并可以责令暂停相关业务、停业整顿、吊销相关业务许可证或者吊销营业执照。法律、行政法规另有规定的，依照其规定。

en.title: Penalties for uncertified critical equipment and products
paragraphs: If, in violation of Article 25 of this Law, a person sells or supplies critical network equipment or specialized cybersecurity products that have not undergone security certification or security testing, have failed security certification, or do not meet security-testing requirements, the relevant competent authority shall order the person to stop selling or supplying them, issue a warning, and confiscate unlawful gains. If there are no unlawful gains or the unlawful gains are less than RMB 100,000, a concurrent fine of not less than RMB 20,000 but not more than RMB 100,000 shall be imposed; if the unlawful gains are RMB 100,000 or more, a concurrent fine of not less than one but not more than five times the unlawful gains shall be imposed. If the circumstances are serious, the authority may also order suspension of relevant business or suspension of operations for rectification, or revoke the relevant business permit or business license. Where laws or administrative regulations provide otherwise, those provisions apply.
en.fullText: If, in violation of Article 25 of this Law, a person sells or supplies critical network equipment or specialized cybersecurity products that have not undergone security certification or security testing, have failed security certification, or do not meet security-testing requirements, the relevant competent authority shall order the person to stop selling or supplying them, issue a warning, and confiscate unlawful gains. If there are no unlawful gains or the unlawful gains are less than RMB 100,000, a concurrent fine of not less than RMB 20,000 but not more than RMB 100,000 shall be imposed; if the unlawful gains are RMB 100,000 or more, a concurrent fine of not less than one but not more than five times the unlawful gains shall be imposed. If the circumstances are serious, the authority may also order suspension of relevant business or suspension of operations for rectification, or revoke the relevant business permit or business license. Where laws or administrative regulations provide otherwise, those provisions apply.

title: Penalties for violating real-name information duties
summary: Editorial overview of Article 64: penalties for violating real-name information duties. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 网络运营者违反本法第二十六条第一款规定，未要求用户提供真实身份信息，或者对不提供真实身份信息的用户提供相关服务的，由有关主管部门责令改正；拒不改正或者情节严重的，处五万元以上五十万元以下罚款，并可以责令暂停相关业务、停业整顿、关闭网站或者应用程序、吊销相关业务许可证或者吊销营业执照，对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员处一万元以上十万元以下罚款。
fullText: 网络运营者违反本法第二十六条第一款规定，未要求用户提供真实身份信息，或者对不提供真实身份信息的用户提供相关服务的，由有关主管部门责令改正；拒不改正或者情节严重的，处五万元以上五十万元以下罚款，并可以责令暂停相关业务、停业整顿、关闭网站或者应用程序、吊销相关业务许可证或者吊销营业执照，对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员处一万元以上十万元以下罚款。

en.title: Penalties for violating real-name information duties
paragraphs: If a network operator violates Article 26, paragraph 1, of this Law by failing to require users to provide authentic identity information or by providing relevant services to users who do not provide authentic identity information, the relevant competent authority shall order correction. If the operator refuses to make corrections or the circumstances are serious, a fine of not less than RMB 50,000 but not more than RMB 500,000 shall be imposed; the authority may also order suspension of relevant business or suspension of operations for rectification, close the website or application, or revoke the relevant business permit or business license; and the directly responsible persons in charge and other directly responsible personnel shall each be fined not less than RMB 10,000 but not more than RMB 100,000.
en.fullText: If a network operator violates Article 26, paragraph 1, of this Law by failing to require users to provide authentic identity information or by providing relevant services to users who do not provide authentic identity information, the relevant competent authority shall order correction. If the operator refuses to make corrections or the circumstances are serious, a fine of not less than RMB 50,000 but not more than RMB 500,000 shall be imposed; the authority may also order suspension of relevant business or suspension of operations for rectification, close the website or application, or revoke the relevant business permit or business license; and the directly responsible persons in charge and other directly responsible personnel shall each be fined not less than RMB 10,000 but not more than RMB 100,000.

title: Penalties for unlawful security assessments or disclosures
summary: Editorial overview of Article 65: penalties for unlawful security assessments or disclosures. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 违反本法第二十八条规定，开展网络安全认证、检测、风险评估等活动，或者向社会发布系统漏洞、计算机病毒、网络攻击、网络侵入等网络安全信息的，由有关主管部门责令改正，给予警告，可以处一万元以上十万元以下罚款；拒不改正或者情节严重的，处十万元以上一百万元以下罚款，并可以责令暂停相关业务、停业整顿、关闭网站或者应用程序、吊销相关业务许可证或者吊销营业执照，对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员处一万元以上十万元以下罚款。
有前款行为，造成本法第六十一条第三款规定的后果的，依照该款规定处罚。
fullText:
违反本法第二十八条规定，开展网络安全认证、检测、风险评估等活动，或者向社会发布系统漏洞、计算机病毒、网络攻击、网络侵入等网络安全信息的，由有关主管部门责令改正，给予警告，可以处一万元以上十万元以下罚款；拒不改正或者情节严重的，处十万元以上一百万元以下罚款，并可以责令暂停相关业务、停业整顿、关闭网站或者应用程序、吊销相关业务许可证或者吊销营业执照，对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员处一万元以上十万元以下罚款。

有前款行为，造成本法第六十一条第三款规定的后果的，依照该款规定处罚。

en.title: Penalties for unlawful security assessments or disclosures
paragraphs: If a person violates Article 28 of this Law by conducting cybersecurity certification, testing, or risk-assessment activities or publicly releasing cybersecurity information concerning system vulnerabilities, computer viruses, cyberattacks, or network intrusions, the relevant competent authority shall order correction and issue a warning and may impose a fine of not less than RMB 10,000 but not more than RMB 100,000. If the person refuses to make corrections or the circumstances are serious, a fine of not less than RMB 100,000 but not more than RMB 1 million shall be imposed; the authority may also order suspension of relevant business or suspension of operations for rectification, close the website or application, or revoke the relevant business permit or business license; and the directly responsible persons in charge and other directly responsible personnel shall each be fined not less than RMB 10,000 but not more than RMB 100,000.
If conduct described in the preceding paragraph causes consequences prescribed in Article 61, paragraph 3, of this Law, penalties shall be imposed in accordance with that paragraph.
en.fullText:
If a person violates Article 28 of this Law by conducting cybersecurity certification, testing, or risk-assessment activities or publicly releasing cybersecurity information concerning system vulnerabilities, computer viruses, cyberattacks, or network intrusions, the relevant competent authority shall order correction and issue a warning and may impose a fine of not less than RMB 10,000 but not more than RMB 100,000. If the person refuses to make corrections or the circumstances are serious, a fine of not less than RMB 100,000 but not more than RMB 1 million shall be imposed; the authority may also order suspension of relevant business or suspension of operations for rectification, close the website or application, or revoke the relevant business permit or business license; and the directly responsible persons in charge and other directly responsible personnel shall each be fined not less than RMB 10,000 but not more than RMB 100,000.

If conduct described in the preceding paragraph causes consequences prescribed in Article 61, paragraph 3, of this Law, penalties shall be imposed in accordance with that paragraph.

title: Penalties for cyberattacks and enabling conduct
summary: Editorial overview of Article 66: penalties for cyberattacks and enabling conduct. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 违反本法第二十九条规定，从事危害网络安全的活动，或者提供专门用于从事危害网络安全活动的程序、工具，或者为他人从事危害网络安全的活动提供技术支持、广告推广、支付结算等帮助，尚不构成犯罪的，由公安机关没收违法所得，处五日以下拘留，可以并处五万元以上五十万元以下罚款；情节较重的，处五日以上十五日以下拘留，可以并处十万元以上一百万元以下罚款。
单位有前款行为的，由公安机关没收违法所得，处十万元以上一百万元以下罚款，并对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员依照前款规定处罚。
违反本法第二十九条规定，受到治安管理处罚的人员，五年内不得从事网络安全管理和网络运营关键岗位的工作；受到刑事处罚的人员，终身不得从事网络安全管理和网络运营关键岗位的工作。
fullText:
违反本法第二十九条规定，从事危害网络安全的活动，或者提供专门用于从事危害网络安全活动的程序、工具，或者为他人从事危害网络安全的活动提供技术支持、广告推广、支付结算等帮助，尚不构成犯罪的，由公安机关没收违法所得，处五日以下拘留，可以并处五万元以上五十万元以下罚款；情节较重的，处五日以上十五日以下拘留，可以并处十万元以上一百万元以下罚款。

单位有前款行为的，由公安机关没收违法所得，处十万元以上一百万元以下罚款，并对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员依照前款规定处罚。

违反本法第二十九条规定，受到治安管理处罚的人员，五年内不得从事网络安全管理和网络运营关键岗位的工作；受到刑事处罚的人员，终身不得从事网络安全管理和网络运营关键岗位的工作。

en.title: Penalties for cyberattacks and enabling conduct
paragraphs: If a person violates Article 29 of this Law by engaging in activities that endanger cybersecurity, providing programs or tools specifically used for activities that endanger cybersecurity, or providing technical support, advertising or promotion, payment settlement, or other assistance for another person's activities that endanger cybersecurity, and the conduct does not constitute a crime, the public security organ shall confiscate unlawful gains and impose detention for not more than five days and may concurrently impose a fine of not less than RMB 50,000 but not more than RMB 500,000. If the circumstances are relatively serious, detention for not less than five but not more than fifteen days shall be imposed, and a concurrent fine of not less than RMB 100,000 but not more than RMB 1 million may be imposed.

If an entity engages in conduct described in the preceding paragraph, the public security organ shall confiscate unlawful gains and impose a fine of not less than RMB 100,000 but not more than RMB 1 million, and shall punish the directly responsible persons in charge and other directly responsible personnel in accordance with the preceding paragraph.

A person who violates Article 29 of this Law and receives a public-security administrative penalty shall not work in cybersecurity management or in a critical network-operations position for five years; a person who receives a criminal penalty shall be barred for life from such work.
en.fullText:
If a person violates Article 29 of this Law by engaging in activities that endanger cybersecurity, providing programs or tools specifically used for activities that endanger cybersecurity, or providing technical support, advertising or promotion, payment settlement, or other assistance for another person's activities that endanger cybersecurity, and the conduct does not constitute a crime, the public security organ shall confiscate unlawful gains and impose detention for not more than five days and may concurrently impose a fine of not less than RMB 50,000 but not more than RMB 500,000. If the circumstances are relatively serious, detention for not less than five but not more than fifteen days shall be imposed, and a concurrent fine of not less than RMB 100,000 but not more than RMB 1 million may be imposed.

If an entity engages in conduct described in the preceding paragraph, the public security organ shall confiscate unlawful gains and impose a fine of not less than RMB 100,000 but not more than RMB 1 million, and shall punish the directly responsible persons in charge and other directly responsible personnel in accordance with the preceding paragraph.

A person who violates Article 29 of this Law and receives a public-security administrative penalty shall not work in cybersecurity management or in a critical network-operations position for five years; a person who receives a criminal penalty shall be barred for life from such work.

title: Penalties for procurement without required security review
summary: Editorial overview of Article 67: penalties for procurement without required security review. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 关键信息基础设施的运营者违反本法第三十七条规定，使用未经安全审查或者安全审查未通过的网络产品或者服务的，由有关主管部门责令限期改正、停止使用、消除对国家安全的影响，处采购金额一倍以上十倍以下罚款，对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员处一万元以上十万元以下罚款。
fullText: 关键信息基础设施的运营者违反本法第三十七条规定，使用未经安全审查或者安全审查未通过的网络产品或者服务的，由有关主管部门责令限期改正、停止使用、消除对国家安全的影响，处采购金额一倍以上十倍以下罚款，对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员处一万元以上十万元以下罚款。

en.title: Penalties for procurement without required security review
paragraphs: If a critical information infrastructure operator violates Article 37 of this Law by using a network product or service that has not undergone security review or has failed security review, the relevant competent authority shall order correction within a prescribed time limit, cessation of use, and elimination of the effect on national security; impose a fine of not less than one but not more than ten times the procurement amount; and impose a fine of not less than RMB 10,000 but not more than RMB 100,000 on each directly responsible person in charge and other directly responsible personnel.
en.fullText: If a critical information infrastructure operator violates Article 37 of this Law by using a network product or service that has not undergone security review or has failed security review, the relevant competent authority shall order correction within a prescribed time limit, cessation of use, and elimination of the effect on national security; impose a fine of not less than one but not more than ten times the procurement amount; and impose a fine of not less than RMB 10,000 but not more than RMB 100,000 on each directly responsible person in charge and other directly responsible personnel.

title: Penalties for criminal websites, groups, and information
summary: Editorial overview of Article 68: penalties for criminal websites, groups, and information. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 违反本法第四十八条规定，设立用于实施违法犯罪活动的网站、通讯群组，或者利用网络发布涉及实施违法犯罪活动的信息，尚不构成犯罪的，由公安机关处五日以下拘留，可以并处一万元以上十万元以下罚款；情节较重的，处五日以上十五日以下拘留，可以并处五万元以上五十万元以下罚款。关闭用于实施违法犯罪活动的网站、通讯群组。
单位有前款行为的，由公安机关处十万元以上五十万元以下罚款，并对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员依照前款规定处罚。
fullText:
违反本法第四十八条规定，设立用于实施违法犯罪活动的网站、通讯群组，或者利用网络发布涉及实施违法犯罪活动的信息，尚不构成犯罪的，由公安机关处五日以下拘留，可以并处一万元以上十万元以下罚款；情节较重的，处五日以上十五日以下拘留，可以并处五万元以上五十万元以下罚款。关闭用于实施违法犯罪活动的网站、通讯群组。

单位有前款行为的，由公安机关处十万元以上五十万元以下罚款，并对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员依照前款规定处罚。

en.title: Penalties for criminal websites, groups, and information
paragraphs: If a person violates Article 48 of this Law by establishing a website or communication group for unlawful or criminal activities, or by using a network to publish information concerning unlawful or criminal activities, and the conduct does not constitute a crime, the public security organ shall impose detention for not more than five days and may concurrently impose a fine of not less than RMB 10,000 but not more than RMB 100,000. If the circumstances are relatively serious, detention for not less than five but not more than fifteen days shall be imposed, and a concurrent fine of not less than RMB 50,000 but not more than RMB 500,000 may be imposed. The website or communication group used for unlawful or criminal activities shall be closed.

If an entity engages in conduct described in the preceding paragraph, the public security organ shall impose a fine of not less than RMB 100,000 but not more than RMB 500,000 on the entity and shall punish the directly responsible persons in charge and other directly responsible personnel in accordance with the preceding paragraph.
en.fullText:
If a person violates Article 48 of this Law by establishing a website or communication group for unlawful or criminal activities, or by using a network to publish information concerning unlawful or criminal activities, and the conduct does not constitute a crime, the public security organ shall impose detention for not more than five days and may concurrently impose a fine of not less than RMB 10,000 but not more than RMB 100,000. If the circumstances are relatively serious, detention for not less than five but not more than fifteen days shall be imposed, and a concurrent fine of not less than RMB 50,000 but not more than RMB 500,000 may be imposed. The website or communication group used for unlawful or criminal activities shall be closed.

If an entity engages in conduct described in the preceding paragraph, the public security organ shall impose a fine of not less than RMB 100,000 but not more than RMB 500,000 on the entity and shall punish the directly responsible persons in charge and other directly responsible personnel in accordance with the preceding paragraph.

title: Penalties for failures to handle prohibited information
summary: Editorial overview of Article 69: penalties for failures to handle prohibited information. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 网络运营者违反本法第四十九条规定，对法律、行政法规禁止发布或者传输的信息未停止传输、采取消除等处置措施、保存有关记录、向有关主管部门报告，或者违反本法第五十二条规定，不按照有关部门的要求对法律、行政法规禁止发布或者传输的信息停止传输、采取消除等处置措施、保存有关记录的，由有关主管部门责令改正，给予警告、予以通报，可以处五万元以上五十万元以下罚款；拒不改正或者情节严重的，处五十万元以上二百万元以下罚款，并可以责令暂停相关业务、停业整顿、关闭网站或者应用程序、吊销相关业务许可证或者吊销营业执照，对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员处五万元以上二十万元以下罚款。
有前款行为，造成特别严重影响、特别严重后果的，由有关主管部门处二百万元以上一千万元以下罚款，责令暂停相关业务、停业整顿、关闭网站或者应用程序、吊销相关业务许可证或者吊销营业执照，对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员处二十万元以上一百万元以下罚款。
电子信息发送服务提供者、应用软件下载服务提供者，不履行本法第五十条第二款规定的安全管理义务的，依照前两款规定处罚。
fullText:
网络运营者违反本法第四十九条规定，对法律、行政法规禁止发布或者传输的信息未停止传输、采取消除等处置措施、保存有关记录、向有关主管部门报告，或者违反本法第五十二条规定，不按照有关部门的要求对法律、行政法规禁止发布或者传输的信息停止传输、采取消除等处置措施、保存有关记录的，由有关主管部门责令改正，给予警告、予以通报，可以处五万元以上五十万元以下罚款；拒不改正或者情节严重的，处五十万元以上二百万元以下罚款，并可以责令暂停相关业务、停业整顿、关闭网站或者应用程序、吊销相关业务许可证或者吊销营业执照，对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员处五万元以上二十万元以下罚款。

有前款行为，造成特别严重影响、特别严重后果的，由有关主管部门处二百万元以上一千万元以下罚款，责令暂停相关业务、停业整顿、关闭网站或者应用程序、吊销相关业务许可证或者吊销营业执照，对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员处二十万元以上一百万元以下罚款。

电子信息发送服务提供者、应用软件下载服务提供者，不履行本法第五十条第二款规定的安全管理义务的，依照前两款规定处罚。

en.title: Penalties for failures to handle prohibited information
paragraphs: If a network operator violates Article 49 of this Law by failing to stop transmitting information whose publication or transmission is prohibited by laws or administrative regulations, take measures such as removal, retain relevant records, or report to the relevant competent authorities, or violates Article 52 of this Law by failing, as required by relevant departments, to stop transmitting such prohibited information, take measures such as removal, or retain relevant records, the relevant competent authority shall order correction, issue a warning, and make a public notice, and may impose a fine of not less than RMB 50,000 but not more than RMB 500,000. If the operator refuses to make corrections or the circumstances are serious, a fine of not less than RMB 500,000 but not more than RMB 2 million shall be imposed; the authority may also order suspension of relevant business or suspension of operations for rectification, close the website or application, or revoke the relevant business permit or business license; and the directly responsible persons in charge and other directly responsible personnel shall each be fined not less than RMB 50,000 but not more than RMB 200,000.

If conduct described in the preceding paragraph causes particularly serious effects or consequences, the relevant competent authority shall impose a fine of not less than RMB 2 million but not more than RMB 10 million; order suspension of relevant business or suspension of operations for rectification, close the website or application, or revoke the relevant business permit or business license; and impose a fine of not less than RMB 200,000 but not more than RMB 1 million on each directly responsible person in charge and other directly responsible personnel.

If an electronic-information delivery service provider or application-software download service provider fails to perform the security-management obligations prescribed in Article 50, paragraph 2, of this Law, it shall be punished in accordance with the preceding two paragraphs.
en.fullText:
If a network operator violates Article 49 of this Law by failing to stop transmitting information whose publication or transmission is prohibited by laws or administrative regulations, take measures such as removal, retain relevant records, or report to the relevant competent authorities, or violates Article 52 of this Law by failing, as required by relevant departments, to stop transmitting such prohibited information, take measures such as removal, or retain relevant records, the relevant competent authority shall order correction, issue a warning, and make a public notice, and may impose a fine of not less than RMB 50,000 but not more than RMB 500,000. If the operator refuses to make corrections or the circumstances are serious, a fine of not less than RMB 500,000 but not more than RMB 2 million shall be imposed; the authority may also order suspension of relevant business or suspension of operations for rectification, close the website or application, or revoke the relevant business permit or business license; and the directly responsible persons in charge and other directly responsible personnel shall each be fined not less than RMB 50,000 but not more than RMB 200,000.

If conduct described in the preceding paragraph causes particularly serious effects or consequences, the relevant competent authority shall impose a fine of not less than RMB 2 million but not more than RMB 10 million; order suspension of relevant business or suspension of operations for rectification, close the website or application, or revoke the relevant business permit or business license; and impose a fine of not less than RMB 200,000 but not more than RMB 1 million on each directly responsible person in charge and other directly responsible personnel.

If an electronic-information delivery service provider or application-software download service provider fails to perform the security-management obligations prescribed in Article 50, paragraph 2, of this Law, it shall be punished in accordance with the preceding two paragraphs.

title: Penalties for obstructing inspections or refusing assistance
summary: Editorial overview of Article 70: penalties for obstructing inspections or refusing assistance. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 网络运营者违反本法规定，有下列行为之一的，由有关主管部门责令改正；拒不改正或者情节严重的，处五万元以上五十万元以下罚款，对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员，处一万元以上十万元以下罚款：
（一）拒绝、阻碍有关部门依法实施的监督检查的；
（二）拒不向公安机关、国家安全机关提供技术支持和协助的。
fullText:
网络运营者违反本法规定，有下列行为之一的，由有关主管部门责令改正；拒不改正或者情节严重的，处五万元以上五十万元以下罚款，对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员，处一万元以上十万元以下罚款：

（一）拒绝、阻碍有关部门依法实施的监督检查的；

（二）拒不向公安机关、国家安全机关提供技术支持和协助的。

en.title: Penalties for obstructing inspections or refusing assistance
paragraphs: If a network operator engages in any of the following conduct in violation of this Law, the relevant competent authority shall order correction. If the operator refuses to make corrections or the circumstances are serious, a fine of not less than RMB 50,000 but not more than RMB 500,000 shall be imposed, and the directly responsible persons in charge and other directly responsible personnel shall each be fined not less than RMB 10,000 but not more than RMB 100,000:
(1) refusing or obstructing supervision or inspection lawfully conducted by relevant departments; or
(2) refusing to provide technical support and assistance to public security organs or state security organs.
en.fullText:
If a network operator engages in any of the following conduct in violation of this Law, the relevant competent authority shall order correction. If the operator refuses to make corrections or the circumstances are serious, a fine of not less than RMB 50,000 but not more than RMB 500,000 shall be imposed, and the directly responsible persons in charge and other directly responsible personnel shall each be fined not less than RMB 10,000 but not more than RMB 100,000:

(1) refusing or obstructing supervision or inspection lawfully conducted by relevant departments; or

(2) refusing to provide technical support and assistance to public security organs or state security organs.

title: Penalties under related laws
summary: Editorial overview of Article 71: penalties under related laws. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 有下列行为之一的，依照有关法律、行政法规的规定处理、处罚：
（一）发布或者传输本法第十三条第二款和其他法律、行政法规禁止发布或者传输的信息的；
（二）违反本法第二十四条第三款、第四十三条至第四十五条规定，侵害个人信息权益的；
（三）违反本法第三十九条规定，关键信息基础设施的运营者在境外存储个人信息和重要数据，或者向境外提供个人信息和重要数据的。
违反本法第四十六条规定，窃取或者以其他非法方式获取、非法出售或者非法向他人提供个人信息，尚不构成犯罪的，由公安机关依照有关法律、行政法规的规定处罚。
fullText:
有下列行为之一的，依照有关法律、行政法规的规定处理、处罚：

（一）发布或者传输本法第十三条第二款和其他法律、行政法规禁止发布或者传输的信息的；

（二）违反本法第二十四条第三款、第四十三条至第四十五条规定，侵害个人信息权益的；

（三）违反本法第三十九条规定，关键信息基础设施的运营者在境外存储个人信息和重要数据，或者向境外提供个人信息和重要数据的。

违反本法第四十六条规定，窃取或者以其他非法方式获取、非法出售或者非法向他人提供个人信息，尚不构成犯罪的，由公安机关依照有关法律、行政法规的规定处罚。

en.title: Penalties under related laws
paragraphs: The following conduct shall be handled and punished in accordance with relevant laws and administrative regulations:
(1) publishing or transmitting information whose publication or transmission is prohibited by Article 13, paragraph 2, of this Law or by other laws or administrative regulations;
(2) infringing personal-information rights and interests in violation of Article 24, paragraph 3, or Articles 43 through 45 of this Law; or

(3) a critical information infrastructure operator's storing personal information or important data outside the territory, or providing personal information or important data outside the territory, in violation of Article 39 of this Law.

If a person violates Article 46 of this Law by stealing or otherwise unlawfully obtaining personal information, or by unlawfully selling or providing personal information to another person, and the conduct does not constitute a crime, the public security organ shall impose penalties in accordance with relevant laws and administrative regulations.
en.fullText:
The following conduct shall be handled and punished in accordance with relevant laws and administrative regulations:

(1) publishing or transmitting information whose publication or transmission is prohibited by Article 13, paragraph 2, of this Law or by other laws or administrative regulations;

(2) infringing personal-information rights and interests in violation of Article 24, paragraph 3, or Articles 43 through 45 of this Law; or

(3) a critical information infrastructure operator's storing personal information or important data outside the territory, or providing personal information or important data outside the territory, in violation of Article 39 of this Law.

If a person violates Article 46 of this Law by stealing or otherwise unlawfully obtaining personal information, or by unlawfully selling or providing personal information to another person, and the conduct does not constitute a crime, the public security organ shall impose penalties in accordance with relevant laws and administrative regulations.

title: Accountability for government network operators
summary: Editorial overview of Article 74: accountability for government network operators. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 国家机关政务网络的运营者不履行本法规定的网络安全保护义务的，由其上级机关或者有关机关责令改正；对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员依法给予处分。

title: Accountability for supervisory authorities and personnel
summary: Editorial overview of Article 75: accountability for supervisory authorities and personnel. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 网信部门和有关部门违反本法第三十二条规定，将在履行网络安全保护职责中获取的信息用于其他用途的，对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员依法给予处分。
网信部门和有关部门的工作人员玩忽职守、滥用职权、徇私舞弊，尚不构成犯罪的，依法给予处分。
fullText:
网信部门和有关部门违反本法第三十二条规定，将在履行网络安全保护职责中获取的信息用于其他用途的，对直接负责的主管人员和其他直接责任人员依法给予处分。

网信部门和有关部门的工作人员玩忽职守、滥用职权、徇私舞弊，尚不构成犯罪的，依法给予处分。

en.title: Accountability for supervisory authorities and personnel
paragraphs: If cyberspace administration or another relevant department violates Article 32 of this Law by using information obtained in performing cybersecurity-protection responsibilities for another purpose, the directly responsible persons in charge and other directly responsible personnel shall be disciplined in accordance with law.

If a staff member of cyberspace administration or another relevant department neglects duties, abuses authority, or engages in favoritism or malpractice, and the conduct does not constitute a crime, the staff member shall be disciplined in accordance with law.
en.fullText:
If cyberspace administration or another relevant department violates Article 32 of this Law by using information obtained in performing cybersecurity-protection responsibilities for another purpose, the directly responsible persons in charge and other directly responsible personnel shall be disciplined in accordance with law.

If a staff member of cyberspace administration or another relevant department neglects duties, abuses authority, or engages in favoritism or malpractice, and the conduct does not constitute a crime, the staff member shall be disciplined in accordance with law.

en.title: Civil, public-security, and criminal liability
paragraphs: A person who violates this Law and causes harm to another person shall bear civil liability in accordance with law.

If a violation of this Law constitutes a public-security administration violation, a public-security administrative penalty shall be imposed in accordance with law; if it constitutes a crime, criminal responsibility shall be pursued in accordance with law.

title: Liability and sanctions for overseas actors
summary: Editorial overview of Article 77: liability and sanctions for overseas actors. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 境外的机构、组织、个人从事危害中华人民共和国网络安全的活动的，依法追究法律责任；造成严重后果的，国务院公安部门和有关部门并可以决定对该机构、组织、个人采取冻结财产或者其他必要的制裁措施。
fullText: 境外的机构、组织、个人从事危害中华人民共和国网络安全的活动的，依法追究法律责任；造成严重后果的，国务院公安部门和有关部门并可以决定对该机构、组织、个人采取冻结财产或者其他必要的制裁措施。

en.title: Liability and sanctions for overseas actors
paragraphs: Overseas institutions, organizations, and individuals that engage in activities endangering the cybersecurity of the People's Republic of China shall be held legally responsible in accordance with law. If serious consequences are caused, the public security department of the State Council and other relevant departments may also decide to take measures against the institution, organization, or individual, including freezing property or imposing other necessary sanctions.
en.fullText: Overseas institutions, organizations, and individuals that engage in activities endangering the cybersecurity of the People's Republic of China shall be held legally responsible in accordance with law. If serious consequences are caused, the public security department of the State Council and other relevant departments may also decide to take measures against the institution, organization, or individual, including freezing property or imposing other necessary sanctions.

title: Statutory definitions
summary: Editorial overview of Article 78: statutory definitions. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 本法下列用语的含义：
（一）网络，是指由计算机或者其他信息终端及相关设备组成的按照一定的规则和程序对信息进行收集、存储、传输、交换、处理的系统。
（二）网络安全，是指通过采取必要措施，防范对网络的攻击、侵入、干扰、破坏和非法使用以及意外事故，使网络处于稳定可靠运行的状态，以及保障网络数据的完整性、保密性、可用性的能力。
（三）网络运营者，是指网络的所有者、管理者和网络服务提供者。
（四）网络数据，是指通过网络收集、存储、传输、处理和产生的各种电子数据。
（五）个人信息，是指以电子或者其他方式记录的能够单独或者与其他信息结合识别自然人个人身份的各种信息，包括但不限于自然人的姓名、出生日期、身份证件号码、个人生物识别信息、住址、电话号码等。
fullText:
本法下列用语的含义：

（一）网络，是指由计算机或者其他信息终端及相关设备组成的按照一定的规则和程序对信息进行收集、存储、传输、交换、处理的系统。

（二）网络安全，是指通过采取必要措施，防范对网络的攻击、侵入、干扰、破坏和非法使用以及意外事故，使网络处于稳定可靠运行的状态，以及保障网络数据的完整性、保密性、可用性的能力。

（三）网络运营者，是指网络的所有者、管理者和网络服务提供者。

（四）网络数据，是指通过网络收集、存储、传输、处理和产生的各种电子数据。

（五）个人信息，是指以电子或者其他方式记录的能够单独或者与其他信息结合识别自然人个人身份的各种信息，包括但不限于自然人的姓名、出生日期、身份证件号码、个人生物识别信息、住址、电话号码等。

en.title: Statutory definitions
paragraphs: For purposes of this Law, the following terms have the meanings set out below:

(1) “network” means a system composed of computers or other information terminals and related equipment that collects, stores, transmits, exchanges, and processes information according to specified rules and procedures.

(2) “cybersecurity” means the capacity, through necessary measures, to prevent attacks, intrusions, interference, destruction, unlawful use, and accidents affecting a network; keep the network operating in a stable and reliable state; and preserve the integrity, confidentiality, and availability of network data.
(3) “network operator” means a network's owner or administrator or a network-service provider.
(4) “network data” means all types of electronic data collected, stored, transmitted, processed, or generated through networks.

(5) “personal information” means all types of information, recorded electronically or by other means, that can identify a natural person's identity either alone or in combination with other information, including but not limited to the natural person's name, date of birth, identification-document number, biometric information, address, and telephone number.
en.fullText:
For purposes of this Law, the following terms have the meanings set out below:

(1) “network” means a system composed of computers or other information terminals and related equipment that collects, stores, transmits, exchanges, and processes information according to specified rules and procedures.

(2) “cybersecurity” means the capacity, through necessary measures, to prevent attacks, intrusions, interference, destruction, unlawful use, and accidents affecting a network; keep the network operating in a stable and reliable state; and preserve the integrity, confidentiality, and availability of network data.

(3) “network operator” means a network's owner or administrator or a network-service provider.

(4) “network data” means all types of electronic data collected, stored, transmitted, processed, or generated through networks.

(5) “personal information” means all types of information, recorded electronically or by other means, that can identify a natural person's identity either alone or in combination with other information, including but not limited to the natural person's name, date of birth, identification-document number, biometric information, address, and telephone number.

title: Networks involving state secrets
summary: Editorial overview of Article 79: networks involving state secrets. The complete current official Chinese provision is stored below.
paragraphs: 存储、处理涉及国家秘密信息的网络的运行安全保护，除应当遵守本法外，还应当遵守保密法律、行政法规的规定。
fullText: 存储、处理涉及国家秘密信息的网络的运行安全保护，除应当遵守本法外，还应当遵守保密法律、行政法规的规定。

en.title: Networks involving state secrets
paragraphs: The operational security protection of networks that store or process information involving State secrets shall comply with this Law as well as laws and administrative regulations concerning confidentiality.
en.fullText: The operational security protection of networks that store or process information involving State secrets shall comply with this Law as well as laws and administrative regulations concerning confidentiality.

title: Security management, technical safeguards, and primary responsibility
summary: Requires network data processors to establish security-management systems, use specified and other necessary technical measures, respond to incidents, and assume primary responsibility for the security of processed network data.
paragraphs: 网络数据处理者应当依照法律、行政法规的规定和国家标准的强制性要求，在网络安全等级保护的基础上，加强网络数据安全防护，建立健全网络数据安全管理制度，采取加密、备份、访问控制、安全认证等技术措施和其他必要措施，保护网络数据免遭篡改、破坏、泄露或者非法获取、非法利用，处置网络数据安全事件，防范针对和利用网络数据实施的违法犯罪活动，并对所处理网络数据的安全承担主体责任。
fullText: 网络数据处理者应当依照法律、行政法规的规定和国家标准的强制性要求，在网络安全等级保护的基础上，加强网络数据安全防护，建立健全网络数据安全管理制度，采取加密、备份、访问控制、安全认证等技术措施和其他必要措施，保护网络数据免遭篡改、破坏、泄露或者非法获取、非法利用，处置网络数据安全事件，防范针对和利用网络数据实施的违法犯罪活动，并对所处理网络数据的安全承担主体责任。

en.title: Security management, technical safeguards, and primary responsibility
paragraphs: Network data processors shall, in accordance with laws, administrative regulations, and mandatory requirements of national standards, strengthen network data security protection on the basis of the multi-level protection scheme for cybersecurity; establish and improve network data security management systems; adopt technical measures such as encryption, backup, access control, and security authentication, together with other necessary measures; protect network data against tampering, destruction, leakage, or unlawful acquisition or use; handle network data security incidents; prevent illegal and criminal activities carried out against or by using network data; and assume primary responsibility for the security of the network data they process.
en.fullText: Network data processors shall, in accordance with laws, administrative regulations, and mandatory requirements of national standards, strengthen network data security protection on the basis of the multi-level protection scheme for cybersecurity; establish and improve network data security management systems; adopt technical measures such as encryption, backup, access control, and security authentication, together with other necessary measures; protect network data against tampering, destruction, leakage, or unlawful acquisition or use; handle network data security incidents; prevent illegal and criminal activities carried out against or by using network data; and assume primary responsibility for the security of the network data they process.
en.note: Editorial reference translation; not an official translation. The official Chinese text controls.
